content:
VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator — Claude Code Prompt
What to build
Build a full-stack web application that scrapes, normalizes, and aggregates startup portfolio data from VC firms and accelerator websites into a single searchable database. Think "Booking.com for startup portfolios."
Tech stack
* Backend: Python + FastAPI
* Scraping: Playwright (for JS-rendered pages) + BeautifulSoup (for static pages)
* LLM extraction: Use Claude API (Anthropic SDK) to extract structured data from messy HTML — this is the secret weapon for handling wildly different portfolio page formats without writing a custom parser per site
* Database: SQLite with SQLAlchemy ORM (easy to swap to Postgres later)
* Frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
* Scheduling: APScheduler for periodic re-scraping
Project structure
vc-aggregator/ ├── backend/ │   ├── main.py                  # FastAPI app entry point │   ├── config.py                # Settings, API keys, DB path │   ├── database/ │   │   ├── models.py            # SQLAlchemy models │   │   ├── connection.py        # DB engine + session │   │   └── seed_sources.py      # Seed list of VC/accelerator URLs │   ├── scraper/ │   │   ├── engine.py            # Core scraping logic (Playwright + BS4) │   │   ├── extractor.py         # Claude API-powered data extraction │   │   ├── normalizer.py        # Clean + deduplicate data │   │   └── scheduler.py         # APScheduler cron jobs │   ├── api/ │   │   ├── routes.py            # REST endpoints │   │   └── schemas.py           # Pydantic request/response models │   └── requirements.txt ├── frontend/ │   ├── app/ │   │   ├── page.tsx             # Main search/browse page │   │   ├── company/[id]/page.tsx # Company detail page │   │   ├── investors/page.tsx   # Browse by investor │   │   └── layout.tsx │   ├── components/ │   │   ├── SearchBar.tsx │   │   ├── CompanyCard.tsx │   │   ├── FilterSidebar.tsx │   │   ├── InvestorGrid.tsx │   │   └── DataTable.tsx │   └── package.json └── README.md 
Database schema
-- The investors (VCs, accelerators) CREATE TABLE investors (     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,     name TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- "Sequoia Capital"     type TEXT NOT NULL,                    -- "vc" | "accelerator" | "angel" | "corporate"     website TEXT,                          -- "https://sequoiacap.com"     portfolio_url TEXT NOT NULL,           -- direct URL to their portfolio page     logo_url TEXT,     description TEXT,     location TEXT,     last_scraped_at TIMESTAMP,     scrape_status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',  -- "pending" | "success" | "failed"     created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  -- The startups/companies in portfolios CREATE TABLE companies (     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,     name TEXT NOT NULL,     slug TEXT UNIQUE,                      -- URL-friendly name     website TEXT,     description TEXT,                      -- one-liner or short description     long_description TEXT,     sector TEXT,                           -- "fintech", "healthtech", "saas", etc.     stage TEXT,                            -- "pre-seed", "seed", "series-a", etc.     founded_year INTEGER,     location TEXT,     employee_count TEXT,                   -- range like "11-50"     logo_url TEXT,     status TEXT DEFAULT 'active',          -- "active" | "acquired" | "dead" | "ipo"     created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP );  -- Many-to-many: which investor backed which company CREATE TABLE investments (     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,     investor_id INTEGER REFERENCES investors(id),     company_id INTEGER REFERENCES companies(id),     investment_date TEXT,                  -- may be approximate     round_type TEXT,                       -- "seed", "series-a", etc.     amount TEXT,                           -- often not public, store as text     is_lead BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,     source_url TEXT,                       -- where we scraped this from     first_seen_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     last_confirmed_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,     UNIQUE(investor_id, company_id, round_type) );  -- Track scraping runs for debugging CREATE TABLE scrape_logs (     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,     investor_id INTEGER REFERENCES investors(id),     started_at TIMESTAMP,     finished_at TIMESTAMP,     status TEXT,                           -- "success" | "failed" | "partial"     companies_found INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     companies_new INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     companies_updated INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     error_message TEXT ); 
Seed data — initial list of sources to scrape
Start with these portfolio pages. Store them in a JSON seed file and load into the investors table:
[     {"name": "Y Combinator", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.ycombinator.com/companies", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "Techstars", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.techstars.com/portfolio", "location": "Boulder, CO"},     {"name": "500 Global", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://500.co/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "Sequoia Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.sequoiacap.com/our-companies/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "a16z", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://a16z.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "Accel", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.accel.com/portfolio", "location": "Palo Alto, CA"},     {"name": "Benchmark", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.benchmark.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "First Round Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://firstround.com/companies/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "Greylock", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://greylock.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "Lightspeed Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://lsvp.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "Founders Fund", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://foundersfund.com/portfolio/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "Bessemer Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.bvp.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},     {"name": "NEA", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.nea.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "Index Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.indexventures.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},     {"name": "GV (Google Ventures)", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.gv.com/portfolio/", "location": "Mountain View, CA"},     {"name": "Kleiner Perkins", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.kleinerperkins.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},     {"name": "Union Square Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.usv.com/portfolio/", "location": "New York, NY"},     {"name": "Antler", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.antler.co/portfolio", "location": "Singapore"},     {"name": "Entrepreneur First", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.joinef.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},     {"name": "Plug and Play", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.plugandplaytechcenter.com/portfolio/", "location": "Sunnyvale, CA"} ] 
Core scraping logic — the Claude-powered extraction
This is the key innovation. Instead of writing a custom scraper for each site, use Claude to extract structured data from raw HTML. Here's the approach:
In scraper/engine.py:
1. Use Playwright to load the portfolio page (handles JS-rendered content)
2. Wait for the page to fully render (wait for network idle)
3. Extract the page's HTML content
4. If the page is very long or has pagination, handle scrolling/pagination to get all companies
5. Chunk the HTML if it's too large for a single Claude API call (split by logical sections)
In scraper/extractor.py:
Send the raw HTML to Claude API with this system prompt:
You are a data extraction engine. You will receive HTML from a venture capital or accelerator portfolio page.  Extract ALL companies/startups listed on this page into a JSON array.  For each company, extract whatever fields are available: - name (required) - website_url - description (short, one-liner if available) - sector/industry - stage (pre-seed, seed, series-a, series-b, etc.) - location - founded_year - logo_url - status (active, acquired, dead, ipo — default to "active" if not specified)  Rules: - Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown formatting, no explanation - If a field is not available, omit it (don't set null) - Normalize sectors to lowercase (e.g., "fintech", "healthtech", "saas", "ai", "devtools", "biotech", "edtech", "climate", "crypto", "ecommerce", "marketplace") - Normalize stages to: "pre-seed", "seed", "series-a", "series-b", "series-c", "series-d", "growth", "ipo" - Extract ALL companies, not just featured ones - If the page has hundreds of companies, extract every single one  Response format: [   {"name": "CompanyName", "website_url": "https://...", "description": "...", "sector": "...", ...},   ... ] 
Use claude-sonnet-4-20250514 for cost efficiency. Send the HTML as user message content. Parse the JSON response and pass it to the normalizer.
In scraper/normalizer.py:
1. Fuzzy-match incoming companies against existing DB records (use company name + website for matching)
2. If a company already exists, update fields that were previously empty
3. If a company is new, create it
4. Create the investment link between investor and company
5. Log the scrape run results
API endpoints
GET  /api/companies                    — list/search companies      ?q=search_term                    — full-text search on name + description      ?sector=fintech,saas              — filter by sector(s)      ?stage=seed,series-a              — filter by stage(s)      ?investor=Y+Combinator            — filter by investor name      ?location=San+Francisco           — filter by location      ?sort=newest|name|investors_count  — sort order      ?page=1&per_page=50               — pagination  GET  /api/companies/:slug              — single company detail + all its investors  GET  /api/investors                    — list all investors      ?type=vc|accelerator              — filter by type  GET  /api/investors/:id                — single investor detail + full portfolio  GET  /api/stats                        — dashboard stats (total companies, investors, sectors breakdown)  POST /api/scrape/:investor_id          — trigger manual scrape for one investor (admin) POST /api/scrape/all                   — trigger full scrape cycle (admin)  GET  /api/scrape/logs                  — view recent scrape logs (admin) 
Frontend pages
1. Main page (/)
* Large search bar at top (like Booking.com)
* Quick stats: "Tracking X,XXX startups across XX investors"
* Filter sidebar: sector tags, stage pills, investor checkboxes, location
* Results grid: company cards showing name, one-liner, sector badge, stage badge, investor logos
* Infinite scroll or pagination
2. Company detail page (/company/[slug])
* Company name, logo, description
* Key facts: sector, stage, location, founded year, website link
* "Backed by" section showing investor cards
* Similar companies (same sector + stage)
3. Investors page (/investors)
* Grid of investor cards with logo, name, type, portfolio count
* Click through to see full portfolio
4. Investor detail page (/investors/[id])
* Investor info + full portfolio table with search/filter
* Stats: total portfolio companies, sector breakdown (pie chart), stage breakdown
Design direction
* Clean, minimal, professional. Think Linear or Notion aesthetics
* Dark/light mode toggle
* Use shadcn/ui components: Command palette for search, Badge for tags, Card for companies, DataTable for lists
* Responsive — works on mobile
* Fast — use server components where possible, client components only for interactivity
Important implementation details
1. Rate limiting scrapers: Add 2-3 second delays between page loads. Respect robots.txt. Use random user agents.
2. Handling pagination: Many portfolio pages load companies dynamically. Use Playwright's scroll-to-bottom and wait-for-new-content pattern. Some sites (like YC) have their own API endpoints you can discover in the network tab — prefer those.
3. Deduplication: Companies appear across multiple investors. Use website domain as primary dedup key, fall back to fuzzy name matching (use thefuzz library).
4. Error handling: Scraping is inherently flaky. Wrap everything in try/except, log failures per investor, and keep going. A failed scrape for one investor shouldn't block others.
5. Data freshness: Store last_scraped_at per investor. The scheduler should re-scrape each investor weekly. Show "last updated" dates in the UI.
6. Environment variables needed:
   * ANTHROPIC_API_KEY — for Claude-powered extraction
   * DATABASE_URL — defaults to local SQLite
Build order
Phase 1 — Get data flowing:
1. Set up the database models and connection
2. Seed the investors table with the URL list
3. Build the scraper engine (Playwright page loader)
4. Build the Claude extractor
5. Build the normalizer + DB writer
6. Test end-to-end with 3-4 investors manually
Phase 2 — Build the API:
1. FastAPI app with all the endpoints above
2. Full-text search using SQLite FTS5
3. Test with curl/httpie
Phase 3 — Build the frontend:
1. Next.js app with the search page
2. Company cards + filter sidebar
3. Company detail page
4. Investors browsing page
5. Connect everything to the API
Phase 4 — Polish:
1. Add the scheduler for automated re-scraping
2. Add scrape logs viewer
3. Add loading states, error states, empty states
4. Dark mode
5. Deploy instructions (Docker compose)

The directory is truly empty (aside from `.claude`). This is a pure greenfield project. Let me now produce the full implementation plan.

---

## VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator -- Implementation Plan

### Overview

This plan covers a full-stack application that scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages, uses Claude to extract structured company data from raw HTML, stores results in SQLite, and presents them through a Next.js frontend backed by a FastAPI REST API.

---

### Phase 0: Project Scaffolding & Dependencies

**Step 0.1: Create the directory tree**

All work happens inside `/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/`.

```
mkdir -p vc-aggregator/backend/{database,scraper,api}
mkdir -p vc-aggregator/frontend
```

**Step 0.2: `backend/requirements.txt`**

```
fastapi==0.115.6
uvicorn[standard]==0.34.0
sqlalchemy==2.0.36
aiosqlite==0.20.0
anthropic==0.46.0
playwright==1.49.1
beautifulsoup4==4.12.3
apscheduler==3.10.4
thefuzz[speedup]==0.22.1
python-levenshtein==0.26.1
httpx==0.28.1
pydantic==2.10.4
pydantic-settings==2.7.1
```

After creating this file:
```bash
cd vc-aggregator/backend
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
playwright install chromium
```

**Step 0.3: Frontend scaffold**

```bash
cd vc-aggregator
npx create-next-app@14 frontend --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir=no --import-alias="@/*"
cd frontend
npx shadcn-ui@latest init   # choose defaults, slate color, CSS variables
npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card input table badge select dialog tabs separator skeleton switch
npm install @tanstack/react-query lucide-react
```

**Critical consideration:** Next.js 14 with App Router requires Node 18+. The system has Node 22.12.0, so this is fine.

---

### Phase 1: Data Pipeline (Backend Core)

This is the foundation. Everything else depends on it. Build and test each file before moving on.

#### File 1: `backend/config.py`

**Purpose:** Centralized configuration using pydantic-settings.

**Key contents:**
- `DATABASE_URL` defaulting to `sqlite+aiosqlite:///./vc_aggregator.db`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` loaded from environment variable
- `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` defaulting to `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`
- `SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN` = 2.0, `SCRAPE_DELAY_MAX` = 3.5 (seconds between requests)
- `MAX_HTML_TOKENS` = 150000 (safety limit for Claude API input -- Claude Sonnet has a 200K context window, but raw HTML is token-heavy; 150K chars of HTML is roughly 40-60K tokens depending on density)
- `USER_AGENTS` list (5-10 realistic browser user-agent strings)
- `LOG_LEVEL` = "INFO"

**Implementation notes:** Use `pydantic_settings.BaseSettings` with `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env")`. This lets the user drop a `.env` file in `backend/` with their `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

#### File 2: `backend/database/connection.py`

**Purpose:** SQLAlchemy async engine and session factory.

**Key contents:**
- Create `async_engine` using `create_async_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL, echo=False)`
- `async_session_maker` using `async_sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)`
- `async def get_db()` generator for FastAPI dependency injection
- `async def init_db()` that calls `Base.metadata.create_all` and also creates the FTS5 virtual table

**FTS5 setup (critical detail):** SQLAlchemy's `create_all` will NOT create FTS5 virtual tables. After `create_all`, execute raw SQL:

```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies_fts USING fts5(
    name, 
    description, 
    industry, 
    content=companies, 
    content_rowid=id
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ai AFTER INSERT ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.industry);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ad AFTER DELETE ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.industry);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_au AFTER UPDATE ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.industry);
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.industry);
END;
```

**Important:** Since we use async SQLite via `aiosqlite`, the FTS5 DDL must be executed through `async_engine.begin()` using `conn.run_sync()` or raw `await conn.execute(text(...))`. The `aiosqlite` driver supports this, but `run_sync` is safer for DDL.

#### File 3: `backend/database/models.py`

**Purpose:** SQLAlchemy ORM models for the 4 tables.

**Tables and columns:**

**`investors`**
- `id` (Integer, PK, autoincrement)
- `name` (String, not null, unique)
- `type` (String -- "vc", "accelerator", "angel", "corporate")
- `website` (String)
- `portfolio_url` (String, not null) -- the URL to scrape
- `logo_url` (String, nullable)
- `description` (Text, nullable)
- `last_scraped_at` (DateTime, nullable)
- `scrape_frequency_hours` (Integer, default 168 = weekly)
- `is_active` (Boolean, default True)
- `created_at` (DateTime, server_default=func.now())
- `updated_at` (DateTime, onupdate=func.now())

**`companies`**
- `id` (Integer, PK, autoincrement)
- `name` (String, not null)
- `slug` (String, unique, not null) -- URL-safe version of name
- `website` (String, nullable) -- used as dedup key when available
- `domain` (String, nullable) -- extracted from website, used for dedup
- `description` (Text, nullable)
- `industry` (String, nullable)
- `stage` (String, nullable) -- "Seed", "Series A", etc.
- `founded_year` (Integer, nullable)
- `headquarters` (String, nullable)
- `logo_url` (String, nullable)
- `status` (String, default "active") -- "active", "acquired", "defunct"
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (DateTime)

**`investments`** (junction table)
- `id` (Integer, PK)
- `investor_id` (ForeignKey investors.id, not null)
- `company_id` (ForeignKey companies.id, not null)
- `announced_date` (Date, nullable)
- `round_type` (String, nullable) -- "Seed", "Series A", etc.
- `created_at` (DateTime)
- UniqueConstraint on (investor_id, company_id)

**`scrape_logs`**
- `id` (Integer, PK)
- `investor_id` (ForeignKey investors.id)
- `status` (String) -- "success", "partial", "failed"
- `companies_found` (Integer, default 0)
- `companies_added` (Integer, default 0)
- `companies_updated` (Integer, default 0)
- `error_message` (Text, nullable)
- `duration_seconds` (Float, nullable)
- `started_at` (DateTime)
- `completed_at` (DateTime, nullable)

**Implementation notes:**
- Add a `generate_slug(name)` utility function that lowercases, strips special chars, replaces spaces with hyphens, and handles collisions by appending `-2`, `-3`, etc.
- Add `__table_args__` with indexes on `companies.domain`, `companies.industry`, `companies.stage`.

#### File 4: `backend/database/seed_sources.py`

**Purpose:** Populate the `investors` table with the 20 seed sources.

**Seed data (key entries):**

| Name | Type | Portfolio URL |
|------|------|---------------|
| Y Combinator | accelerator | https://www.ycombinator.com/companies |
| Techstars | accelerator | https://www.techstars.com/portfolio |
| 500 Global | accelerator | https://500.co/portfolio |
| Sequoia Capital | vc | https://www.sequoiacap.com/our-companies/ |
| Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) | vc | https://a16z.com/portfolio/ |
| Accel | vc | https://www.accel.com/portfolio |
| Benchmark | vc | https://www.benchmark.com/portfolio |
| Greylock Partners | vc | https://greylock.com/portfolio/ |
| Lightspeed Venture Partners | vc | https://lsvp.com/portfolio/ |
| Index Ventures | vc | https://www.indexventures.com/companies/ |
| Founders Fund | vc | https://foundersfund.com/portfolio/ |
| NEA | vc | https://www.nea.com/portfolio |
| Bessemer Venture Partners | vc | https://www.bvp.com/portfolio |
| General Catalyst | vc | https://www.generalcatalyst.com/portfolio |
| Khosla Ventures | vc | https://www.khoslaventures.com/portfolio |
| First Round Capital | vc | https://firstround.com/companies/ |
| Union Square Ventures | vc | https://www.usv.com/portfolio |
| Insight Partners | vc | https://www.insightpartners.com/portfolio/ |
| GV (Google Ventures) | vc | https://www.gv.com/portfolio/ |
| Kleiner Perkins | vc | https://www.kleinerperkins.com/portfolio |

**Implementation:** An async function `seed_investors(session)` that does an upsert (insert or skip if name already exists). Call this from `init_db()`.

**Critical warning:** Some of these URLs may have changed or may block scrapers. The scraper must handle 403/404 gracefully. In particular:
- YC's `/companies` page is a React SPA that loads data via API calls -- Playwright is essential here.
- a16z's portfolio page uses dynamic loading.
- Several sites may require cookie consent dismissal.

#### File 5: `backend/scraper/engine.py`

**Purpose:** The actual HTTP/browser fetching layer.

**Two strategies:**

1. **`fetch_with_playwright(url) -> str`** -- For JS-rendered pages. Launches headless Chromium, navigates to URL, waits for network idle, scrolls to load lazy content, returns `page.content()`.

2. **`fetch_with_httpx(url) -> str`** -- For static pages. Simple GET with random user-agent, returns response text.

**Key implementation details:**

```python
async def fetch_with_playwright(url: str, scroll: bool = True, max_scrolls: int = 50) -> str:
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(
            user_agent=random.choice(USER_AGENTS),
            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080}
        )
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Block images/fonts/media to speed up loading
        await page.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,svg,woff,woff2,ttf,mp4,webm}", 
                         lambda route: route.abort())
        
        await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=60000)
        
        if scroll:
            # Scroll to bottom to trigger lazy loading
            prev_height = 0
            for _ in range(max_scrolls):
                curr_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight")
                if curr_height == prev_height:
                    break
                await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
                await page.wait_for_timeout(1500)
                prev_height = curr_height
        
        html = await page.content()
        await browser.close()
        return html
```

**YC-specific consideration:** YC has thousands of companies. The scroll loop could run indefinitely. Solutions:
1. Set `max_scrolls=50` (reasonable default, loads ~500-1000 companies per scroll session).
2. Better approach: Intercept YC's internal API. YC's company page hits `https://api.ycombinator.com/v0.1/companies` -- if we detect this, switch to direct API consumption. Add a `fetch_yc_api()` special case that paginates through their API. This is more reliable and faster.
3. For the initial build, the scroll approach with a cap is fine. Add YC API integration as a Phase 4 enhancement.

**HTML size problem:** A fully-loaded YC page can be 10MB+ of HTML. Sending this raw to Claude would exceed token limits and cost a fortune. The solution is in `extractor.py` -- we pre-process HTML before sending to Claude.

#### File 6: `backend/scraper/extractor.py`

**Purpose:** Uses Claude API to extract structured company data from HTML.

**This is the most critical file in the project.**

**Strategy for handling large HTML:**

1. **Pre-processing with BeautifulSoup:** Before sending to Claude, strip the HTML down:
   ```python
   def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
       soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, 'html.parser')
       # Remove scripts, styles, nav, footer, header, svg, noscript
       for tag in soup(["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript", "meta", "link"]):
           tag.decompose()
       # Remove all attributes except href, src, alt, class (class helps Claude understand structure)
       for tag in soup.find_all(True):
           allowed = {}
           for attr in ["href", "src", "alt", "class"]:
               if tag.get(attr):
                   allowed[attr] = tag[attr]
           tag.attrs = allowed
       # Get text-heavy HTML
       cleaned = str(soup)
       return cleaned
   ```

2. **Chunking:** If cleaned HTML > `MAX_HTML_TOKENS` chars (~150K), split into chunks. Find natural break points (repeated DOM patterns like `<div class="company-card">` etc.). Send each chunk to Claude separately and merge results.

3. **Claude prompt design:**

```python
EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are a data extraction specialist. Extract ALL company information from this VC/accelerator portfolio page HTML.

For each company found, extract:
- name (required)
- website (URL if available)
- description (brief, 1-2 sentences)
- industry (categorize: "AI/ML", "Fintech", "Healthcare", "SaaS", "E-commerce", "Crypto/Web3", "Developer Tools", "Cybersecurity", "EdTech", "Climate/Energy", "Other")
- stage (if mentioned: "Pre-Seed", "Seed", "Series A", "Series B", "Series C+", "Growth", "Public")
- founded_year (if mentioned)
- headquarters (city/country if mentioned)
- status ("active", "acquired", "defunct" - default to "active")

Return a JSON array. Example:
[
  {
    "name": "Stripe",
    "website": "https://stripe.com",
    "description": "Online payment processing platform",
    "industry": "Fintech",
    "stage": "Growth",
    "founded_year": 2010,
    "headquarters": "San Francisco, CA",
    "status": "active"
  }
]

IMPORTANT: 
- Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown code fences
- If a field is not available, use null
- Extract ALL companies visible in the HTML, not just a sample
"""
```

4. **API call:**
```python
async def extract_companies(html: str, investor_name: str) -> list[dict]:
    cleaned = preprocess_html(html)
    
    # Chunk if needed
    chunks = chunk_html(cleaned, max_chars=120000)
    
    all_companies = []
    client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()  # uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
    
    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        response = await client.messages.create(
            model=settings.ANTHROPIC_MODEL,
            max_tokens=4096,
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Extract companies from this {investor_name} portfolio page (part {i+1}/{len(chunks)}):\n\n{chunk}"
            }],
            system=EXTRACTION_PROMPT
        )
        
        text = response.content[0].text
        companies = json.loads(text)
        all_companies.extend(companies)
    
    return all_companies
```

**Token cost estimation:** A cleaned portfolio page is typically 20K-80K chars (5K-20K tokens). At Claude Sonnet pricing ($3/M input, $15/M output), scraping all 20 investors once costs roughly $0.50-$2.00 total. YC is the outlier -- it could be $5+ if fully loaded.

**Error handling:** Wrap `json.loads` in try/except. If Claude returns malformed JSON, try to fix common issues (trailing commas, markdown fences) before failing. Log the raw response for debugging.

#### File 7: `backend/scraper/normalizer.py`

**Purpose:** Deduplication and data normalization before database insertion.

**Key functions:**

```python
def extract_domain(url: str) -> str | None:
    """https://www.stripe.com/pricing -> stripe.com"""
    if not url:
        return None
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    domain = parsed.netloc.lower()
    if domain.startswith("www."):
        domain = domain[4:]
    return domain or None

def normalize_company_name(name: str) -> str:
    """Strip Inc., Ltd., etc. and normalize whitespace"""
    ...

async def deduplicate_and_upsert(
    session: AsyncSession,
    companies: list[dict],
    investor_id: int
) -> dict:  # returns {"added": N, "updated": N, "skipped": N}
```

**Deduplication strategy (in order of priority):**
1. **Domain match:** If company has a website, extract domain. Look up existing company by domain. If found, link to this investor (create investment record) but don't create duplicate company.
2. **Fuzzy name match:** If no website, use `thefuzz.fuzz.token_sort_ratio(name1, name2) > 85` against existing companies. This handles "Stripe, Inc." vs "Stripe" vs "stripe".
3. **If no match:** Insert new company.

**Upsert logic:** When a match is found, update nullable fields that are currently NULL with new data (fill in gaps), but don't overwrite existing non-null data (first source wins). Always create the `investments` junction record.

#### File 8: `backend/scraper/scheduler.py`

**Purpose:** APScheduler setup for periodic scraping.

**Implementation:**
```python
from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler

scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler()

async def scrape_investor(investor_id: int):
    """Full pipeline: fetch -> extract -> normalize -> store"""
    async with async_session_maker() as session:
        investor = await session.get(Investor, investor_id)
        log = ScrapeLog(investor_id=investor_id, status="running", started_at=datetime.utcnow())
        session.add(log)
        await session.commit()
        
        try:
            html = await fetch_with_playwright(investor.portfolio_url)
            companies = await extract_companies(html, investor.name)
            result = await deduplicate_and_upsert(session, companies, investor_id)
            
            log.status = "success"
            log.companies_found = len(companies)
            log.companies_added = result["added"]
            log.companies_updated = result["updated"]
            investor.last_scraped_at = datetime.utcnow()
        except Exception as e:
            log.status = "failed"
            log.error_message = str(e)[:2000]
        finally:
            log.completed_at = datetime.utcnow()
            log.duration_seconds = (log.completed_at - log.started_at).total_seconds()
            await session.commit()

def setup_scheduler():
    # Add jobs for each active investor based on their scrape_frequency_hours
    # Also add a "check for new investors" job that runs daily
    scheduler.start()
```

**Important:** Don't start the scheduler during development/testing. Gate it behind an env var `ENABLE_SCHEDULER=true`.

**Testing Phase 1:**

After building all Phase 1 files, test the pipeline end-to-end with a single investor:

```python
# test_pipeline.py (manual test script, not automated tests)
async def test():
    await init_db()
    # Pick a simpler portfolio page first (e.g., Union Square Ventures - smaller, cleaner HTML)
    html = await fetch_with_playwright("https://www.usv.com/portfolio")
    companies = await extract_companies(html, "Union Square Ventures")
    print(f"Found {len(companies)} companies")
    for c in companies[:5]:
        print(c)
```

Run this to verify: (a) Playwright works, (b) Claude extracts correctly, (c) data looks reasonable. Then test the full pipeline including DB writes.

---

**Key schemas:**
- `CompanyOut` -- all company fields + list of investor names/IDs
- `CompanyListOut` -- paginated: `items: list[CompanyOut]`, `total: int`, `page: int`, `page_size: int`
- `InvestorOut` -- all investor fields + `portfolio_count: int`
- `InvestorListOut` -- paginated
- `ScrapeLogOut` -- log entry with investor name
- `SearchParams` -- query string, industry filter, stage filter, investor filter, page, page_size
- `ScrapeRequest` -- `investor_id: int` for manual trigger

#### File 10: `backend/api/routes.py`

**Purpose:** FastAPI router with all endpoints.

**Endpoints:**

```
GET  /api/companies              -- list with search, filter, pagination
GET  /api/companies/{slug}       -- single company detail
GET  /api/investors              -- list all investors
GET  /api/investors/{id}         -- investor detail with their companies
GET  /api/investors/{id}/companies -- paginated companies for investor
POST /api/scrape/{investor_id}   -- trigger manual scrape (background task)
GET  /api/scrape/logs            -- paginated scrape logs
GET  /api/stats                  -- dashboard stats (total companies, investors, recent scrapes)
GET  /api/filters                -- available filter values (industries, stages, investors)
```

**Search implementation (FTS5):**

```python
@router.get("/api/companies")
async def list_companies(
    q: str | None = None,
    industry: str | None = None,
    stage: str | None = None,
    investor_id: int | None = None,
    page: int = 1,
    page_size: int = 24,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
):
    if q:
        # FTS5 search
        fts_query = text("""
            SELECT c.* FROM companies c
            JOIN companies_fts fts ON c.id = fts.rowid
            WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query
            ORDER BY rank
        """)
        # Then apply additional filters as WHERE clauses
    else:
        # Regular query with optional filters
        query = select(Company)
        if industry:
            query = query.where(Company.industry == industry)
        if stage:
            query = query.where(Company.stage == stage)
        if investor_id:
            query = query.join(Investment).where(Investment.investor_id == investor_id)
        ...
```

**FTS5 query syntax note:** FTS5 `MATCH` supports prefix queries (`strip*`), phrase queries (`"online payments"`), and boolean operators (`stripe OR paypal`). Expose this naturally -- users typing "ai healthcare" gets tokenized into `ai AND healthcare` by FTS5 by default. No special handling needed.

**Manual scrape endpoint:**

```python
@router.post("/api/scrape/{investor_id}")
async def trigger_scrape(investor_id: int, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    investor = await db.get(Investor, investor_id)
    if not investor:
        raise HTTPException(404, "Investor not found")
    background_tasks.add_task(scrape_investor, investor_id)
    return {"message": f"Scrape started for {investor.name}", "investor_id": investor_id}
```

**CORS note:** Must be configured in `main.py`.

```python
app.include_router(api_router)
```

```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
```

### Potential Issues & Mitigations

| Issue | Impact | Mitigation |
|-------|--------|------------|
| Claude API cost for large pages (YC) | $5+ per full scrape | Pre-process HTML aggressively; consider YC's API directly; cache results |
| Portfolio page structure changes | Scraper returns 0 companies | Claude LLM approach is inherently resilient to layout changes; log warnings when 0 companies found |
| Rate limiting / blocking by sites | 403 errors | Random delays (2-5s), rotate user agents, respect robots.txt, exponential backoff |
| SQLite concurrent writes | Write locks during scrape | Use WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`), scrape one investor at a time |
| Playwright memory usage | OOM on small servers | Close browser after each scrape, don't run concurrent Playwright instances |
| FTS5 not available in SQLite build | Import error | Python's bundled SQLite usually includes FTS5; verify with `SELECT * FROM pragma_compile_options WHERE compile_options LIKE '%FTS5%'` |
| Next.js SSR calling localhost:8000 | Fails in Docker | Use `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` env var; in Docker, use service name `http://backend:8000` |
| Stale data / dedup failures | Duplicate companies | Domain-based dedup is primary; run periodic dedup cleanup job |
| Claude returning invalid JSON | Parse errors | Retry once with explicit "return only valid JSON" instruction; fallback to regex extraction |
| Very large investor portfolios (>1000 companies) | Timeout / memory | Chunk HTML, paginate API calls, set max companies per scrape run |

The directory is truly empty (aside from `.claude`). This is a pure greenfield project. Let me now produce the full implementation plan.

---

## VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator -- Implementation Plan

### Overview

This plan covers a full-stack application that scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages, uses Claude to extract structured company data from raw HTML, stores results in SQLite, and presents them through a Next.js frontend backed by a FastAPI REST API.

---

### Phase 0: Project Scaffolding & Dependencies

**Step 0.1: Create the directory tree**

All work happens inside `/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/`.

```
mkdir -p vc-aggregator/backend/{database,scraper,api}
mkdir -p vc-aggregator/frontend
```

**Step 0.2: `backend/requirements.txt`**

```
fastapi==0.115.6
uvicorn[standard]==0.34.0
sqlalchemy==2.0.36
aiosqlite==0.20.0
anthropic==0.46.0
playwright==1.49.1
beautifulsoup4==4.12.3
apscheduler==3.10.4
thefuzz[speedup]==0.22.1
python-levenshtein==0.26.1
httpx==0.28.1
pydantic==2.10.4
pydantic-settings==2.7.1
```

After creating this file:
```bash
cd vc-aggregator/backend
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
playwright install chromium
```

**Step 0.3: Frontend scaffold**

```bash
cd vc-aggregator
npx create-next-app@14 frontend --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir=no --import-alias="@/*"
cd frontend
npx shadcn-ui@latest init   # choose defaults, slate color, CSS variables
npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card input table badge select dialog tabs separator skeleton switch
npm install @tanstack/react-query lucide-react
```

**Critical consideration:** Next.js 14 with App Router requires Node 18+. The system has Node 22.12.0, so this is fine.

---

### Phase 1: Data Pipeline (Backend Core)

This is the foundation. Everything else depends on it. Build and test each file before moving on.

#### File 1: `backend/config.py`

**Purpose:** Centralized configuration using pydantic-settings.

**Key contents:**
- `DATABASE_URL` defaulting to `sqlite+aiosqlite:///./vc_aggregator.db`
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` loaded from environment variable
- `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` defaulting to `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`
- `SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN` = 2.0, `SCRAPE_DELAY_MAX` = 3.5 (seconds between requests)
- `MAX_HTML_TOKENS` = 150000 (safety limit for Claude API input -- Claude Sonnet has a 200K context window, but raw HTML is token-heavy; 150K chars of HTML is roughly 40-60K tokens depending on density)
- `USER_AGENTS` list (5-10 realistic browser user-agent strings)
- `LOG_LEVEL` = "INFO"

**Implementation notes:** Use `pydantic_settings.BaseSettings` with `model_config = SettingsConfigDict(env_file=".env")`. This lets the user drop a `.env` file in `backend/` with their `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`.

#### File 2: `backend/database/connection.py`

**Purpose:** SQLAlchemy async engine and session factory.

**Key contents:**
- Create `async_engine` using `create_async_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL, echo=False)`
- `async_session_maker` using `async_sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)`
- `async def get_db()` generator for FastAPI dependency injection
- `async def init_db()` that calls `Base.metadata.create_all` and also creates the FTS5 virtual table

**FTS5 setup (critical detail):** SQLAlchemy's `create_all` will NOT create FTS5 virtual tables. After `create_all`, execute raw SQL:

```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies_fts USING fts5(
    name, 
    description, 
    industry, 
    content=companies, 
    content_rowid=id
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ai AFTER INSERT ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.industry);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ad AFTER DELETE ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.industry);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_au AFTER UPDATE ON companies BEGIN
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.industry);
    INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, industry) 
    VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.industry);
END;
```

**Important:** Since we use async SQLite via `aiosqlite`, the FTS5 DDL must be executed through `async_engine.begin()` using `conn.run_sync()` or raw `await conn.execute(text(...))`. The `aiosqlite` driver supports this, but `run_sync` is safer for DDL.

#### File 3: `backend/database/models.py`

**Purpose:** SQLAlchemy ORM models for the 4 tables.

**Tables and columns:**

**`investors`**
- `id` (Integer, PK, autoincrement)
- `name` (String, not null, unique)
- `type` (String -- "vc", "accelerator", "angel", "corporate")
- `website` (String)
- `portfolio_url` (String, not null) -- the URL to scrape
- `logo_url` (String, nullable)
- `description` (Text, nullable)
- `last_scraped_at` (DateTime, nullable)
- `scrape_frequency_hours` (Integer, default 168 = weekly)
- `is_active` (Boolean, default True)
- `created_at` (DateTime, server_default=func.now())
- `updated_at` (DateTime, onupdate=func.now())

**`companies`**
- `id` (Integer, PK, autoincrement)
- `name` (String, not null)
- `slug` (String, unique, not null) -- URL-safe version of name
- `website` (String, nullable) -- used as dedup key when available
- `domain` (String, nullable) -- extracted from website, used for dedup
- `description` (Text, nullable)
- `industry` (String, nullable)
- `stage` (String, nullable) -- "Seed", "Series A", etc.
- `founded_year` (Integer, nullable)
- `headquarters` (String, nullable)
- `logo_url` (String, nullable)
- `status` (String, default "active") -- "active", "acquired", "defunct"
- `created_at`, `updated_at` (DateTime)

**`investments`** (junction table)
- `id` (Integer, PK)
- `investor_id` (ForeignKey investors.id, not null)
- `company_id` (ForeignKey companies.id, not null)
- `announced_date` (Date, nullable)
- `round_type` (String, nullable) -- "Seed", "Series A", etc.
- `created_at` (DateTime)
- UniqueConstraint on (investor_id, company_id)

**`scrape_logs`**
- `id` (Integer, PK)
- `investor_id` (ForeignKey investors.id)
- `status` (String) -- "success", "partial", "failed"
- `companies_found` (Integer, default 0)
- `companies_added` (Integer, default 0)
- `companies_updated` (Integer, default 0)
- `error_message` (Text, nullable)
- `duration_seconds` (Float, nullable)
- `started_at` (DateTime)
- `completed_at` (DateTime, nullable)

**Implementation notes:**
- Add a `generate_slug(name)` utility function that lowercases, strips special chars, replaces spaces with hyphens, and handles collisions by appending `-2`, `-3`, etc.
- Add `__table_args__` with indexes on `companies.domain`, `companies.industry`, `companies.stage`.

#### File 4: `backend/database/seed_sources.py`

**Purpose:** Populate the `investors` table with the 20 seed sources.

**Seed data (key entries):**

| Name | Type | Portfolio URL |
|------|------|---------------|

#### File 5: `backend/scraper/engine.py`

**Purpose:** The actual HTTP/browser fetching layer.

**Two strategies:**

1. **`fetch_with_playwright(url) -> str`** -- For JS-rendered pages. Launches headless Chromium, navigates to URL, waits for network idle, scrolls to load lazy content, returns `page.content()`.

2. **`fetch_with_httpx(url) -> str`** -- For static pages. Simple GET with random user-agent, returns response text.

**Key implementation details:**

```python
async def fetch_with_playwright(url: str, scroll: bool = True, max_scrolls: int = 50) -> str:
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(
            user_agent=random.choice(USER_AGENTS),
            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080}
        )
        page = await context.new_page()
        
        # Block images/fonts/media to speed up loading
        await page.route("**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,svg,woff,woff2,ttf,mp4,webm}", 
                         lambda route: route.abort())
        
        await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=60000)
        
        if scroll:
            # Scroll to bottom to trigger lazy loading
            prev_height = 0
            for _ in range(max_scrolls):
                curr_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight")
                if curr_height == prev_height:
                    break
                await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
                await page.wait_for_timeout(1500)
                prev_height = curr_height
        
        html = await page.content()
        await browser.close()
        return html
```

**YC-specific consideration:** YC has thousands of companies. The scroll loop could run indefinitely. Solutions:
1. Set `max_scrolls=50` (reasonable default, loads ~500-1000 companies per scroll session).
2. Better approach: Intercept YC's internal API. YC's company page hits `https://api.ycombinator.com/v0.1/companies` -- if we detect this, switch to direct API consumption. Add a `fetch_yc_api()` special case that paginates through their API. This is more reliable and faster.
3. For the initial build, the scroll approach with a cap is fine. Add YC API integration as a Phase 4 enhancement.

**HTML size problem:** A fully-loaded YC page can be 10MB+ of HTML. Sending this raw to Claude would exceed token limits and cost a fortune. The solution is in `extractor.py` -- we pre-process HTML before sending to Claude.

#### File 6: `backend/scraper/extractor.py`

**Purpose:** Uses Claude API to extract structured company data from HTML.

**This is the most critical file in the project.**

**Strategy for handling large HTML:**

1. **Pre-processing with BeautifulSoup:** Before sending to Claude, strip the HTML down:
   ```python
   def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
       soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, 'html.parser')
       # Remove scripts, styles, nav, footer, header, svg, noscript
       for tag in soup(["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript", "meta", "link"]):
           tag.decompose()
       # Remove all attributes except href, src, alt, class (class helps Claude understand structure)
       for tag in soup.find_all(True):
           allowed = {}
           for attr in ["href", "src", "alt", "class"]:
               if tag.get(attr):
                   allowed[attr] = tag[attr]
           tag.attrs = allowed
       # Get text-heavy HTML
       cleaned = str(soup)
       return cleaned
   ```

2. **Chunking:** If cleaned HTML > `MAX_HTML_TOKENS` chars (~150K), split into chunks. Find natural break points (repeated DOM patterns like `<div class="company-card">` etc.). Send each chunk to Claude separately and merge results.

3. **Claude prompt design:**

```python
EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are a data extraction specialist. Extract ALL company information from this VC/accelerator portfolio page HTML.

For each company found, extract:
- name (required)
- website (URL if available)
- description (brief, 1-2 sentences)
- industry (categorize: "AI/ML", "Fintech", "Healthcare", "SaaS", "E-commerce", "Crypto/Web3", "Developer Tools", "Cybersecurity", "EdTech", "Climate/Energy", "Other")
- stage (if mentioned: "Pre-Seed", "Seed", "Series A", "Series B", "Series C+", "Growth", "Public")
- founded_year (if mentioned)
- headquarters (city/country if mentioned)
- status ("active", "acquired", "defunct" - default to "active")

Return a JSON array. Example:
[
  {
    "name": "Stripe",
    "website": "https://stripe.com",
    "description": "Online payment processing platform",
    "industry": "Fintech",
    "stage": "Growth",
    "founded_year": 2010,
    "headquarters": "San Francisco, CA",
    "status": "active"
  }
]

IMPORTANT: 
- Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown code fences
- If a field is not available, use null
- Extract ALL companies visible in the HTML, not just a sample
"""
```

4. **API call:**
```python
async def extract_companies(html: str, investor_name: str) -> list[dict]:
    cleaned = preprocess_html(html)
    
    # Chunk if needed
    chunks = chunk_html(cleaned, max_chars=120000)
    
    all_companies = []
    client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()  # uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
    
    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        response = await client.messages.create(
            model=settings.ANTHROPIC_MODEL,
            max_tokens=4096,
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Extract companies from this {investor_name} portfolio page (part {i+1}/{len(chunks)}):\n\n{chunk}"
            }],
            system=EXTRACTION_PROMPT
        )
        
        text = response.content[0].text
        companies = json.loads(text)
        all_companies.extend(companies)
    
    return all_companies
```

**Token cost estimation:** A cleaned portfolio page is typically 20K-80K chars (5K-20K tokens). At Claude Sonnet pricing ($3/M input, $15/M output), scraping all 20 investors once costs roughly $0.50-$2.00 total. YC is the outlier -- it could be $5+ if fully loaded.

**Error handling:** Wrap `json.loads` in try/except. If Claude returns malformed JSON, try to fix common issues (trailing commas, markdown fences) before failing. Log the raw response for debugging.

#### File 7: `backend/scraper/normalizer.py`

**Purpose:** Deduplication and data normalization before database insertion.

**Key functions:**

```python
def extract_domain(url: str) -> str | None:
    """https://www.stripe.com/pricing -> stripe.com"""
    if not url:
        return None
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    domain = parsed.netloc.lower()
    if domain.startswith("www."):
        domain = domain[4:]
    return domain or None

def normalize_company_name(name: str) -> str:
    """Strip Inc., Ltd., etc. and normalize whitespace"""
    ...

async def deduplicate_and_upsert(
    session: AsyncSession,
    companies: list[dict],
    investor_id: int
) -> dict:  # returns {"added": N, "updated": N, "skipped": N}
```

**Deduplication strategy (in order of priority):**
1. **Domain match:** If company has a website, extract domain. Look up existing company by domain. If found, link to this investor (create investment record) but don't create duplicate company.
2. **Fuzzy name match:** If no website, use `thefuzz.fuzz.token_sort_ratio(name1, name2) > 85` against existing companies. This handles "Stripe, Inc." vs "Stripe" vs "stripe".
3. **If no match:** Insert new company.

**Upsert logic:** When a match is found, update nullable fields that are currently NULL with new data (fill in gaps), but don't overwrite existing non-null data (first source wins). Always create the `investments` junction record.

#### File 8: `backend/scraper/scheduler.py`

**Purpose:** APScheduler setup for periodic scraping.

**Implementation:**
```python
from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler

scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler()

async def scrape_investor(investor_id: int):
    """Full pipeline: fetch -> extract -> normalize -> store"""
    async with async_session_maker() as session:
        investor = await session.get(Investor, investor_id)
        log = ScrapeLog(investor_id=investor_id, status="running", started_at=datetime.utcnow())
        session.add(log)
        await session.commit()
        
        try:
            html = await fetch_with_playwright(investor.portfolio_url)
            companies = await extract_companies(html, investor.name)
            result = await deduplicate_and_upsert(session, companies, investor_id)
            
            log.status = "success"
            log.companies_found = len(companies)
            log.companies_added = result["added"]
            log.companies_updated = result["updated"]
            investor.last_scraped_at = datetime.utcnow()
        except Exception as e:
            log.status = "failed"
            log.error_message = str(e)[:2000]
        finally:
            log.completed_at = datetime.utcnow()
            log.duration_seconds = (log.completed_at - log.started_at).total_seconds()
            await session.commit()

def setup_scheduler():
    # Add jobs for each active investor based on their scrape_frequency_hours
    # Also add a "check for new investors" job that runs daily
    scheduler.start()
```

**Important:** Don't start the scheduler during development/testing. Gate it behind an env var `ENABLE_SCHEDULER=true`.

**Search implementation (FTS5):**

```python
@router.get("/api/companies")
async def list_companies(
    q: str | None = None,
    industry: str | None = None,
    stage: str | None = None,
    investor_id: int | None = None,
    page: int = 1,
    page_size: int = 24,
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)
):
    if q:
        # FTS5 search
        fts_query = text("""
            SELECT c.* FROM companies c
            JOIN companies_fts fts ON c.id = fts.rowid
            WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query
            ORDER BY rank
        """)
        # Then apply additional filters as WHERE clauses
    else:
        # Regular query with optional filters
        query = select(Company)
        if industry:
            query = query.where(Company.industry == industry)
        if stage:
            query = query.where(Company.stage == stage)
        if investor_id:
            query = query.join(Investment).where(Investment.investor_id == investor_id)
        ...
```

**FTS5 query syntax note:** FTS5 `MATCH` supports prefix queries (`strip*`), phrase queries (`"online payments"`), and boolean operators (`stripe OR paypal`). Expose this naturally -- users typing "ai healthcare" gets tokenized into `ai AND healthcare` by FTS5 by default. No special handling needed.

**Manual scrape endpoint:**

```python
@router.post("/api/scrape/{investor_id}")
async def trigger_scrape(investor_id: int, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):
    investor = await db.get(Investor, investor_id)
    if not investor:
        raise HTTPException(404, "Investor not found")
    background_tasks.add_task(scrape_investor, investor_id)
    return {"message": f"Scrape started for {investor.name}", "investor_id": investor_id}
```

**CORS note:** Must be configured in `main.py`.

### Potential Issues & Mitigations

| Issue | Impact | Mitigation |
|-------|--------|------------|
| Claude API cost for large pages (YC) | $5+ per full scrape | Pre-process HTML aggressively; consider YC's API directly; cache results |
| Portfolio page structure changes | Scraper returns 0 companies | Claude LLM approach is inherently resilient to layout changes; log warnings when 0 companies found |
| Rate limiting / blocking by sites | 403 errors | Random delays (2-5s), rotate user agents, respect robots.txt, exponential backoff |
| SQLite concurrent writes | Write locks during scrape | Use WAL mode (`PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`), scrape one investor at a time |
| Playwright memory usage | OOM on small servers | Close browser after each scrape, don't run concurrent Playwright instances |
| FTS5 not available in SQLite build | Import error | Python's bundled SQLite usually includes FTS5; verify with `SELECT * FROM pragma_compile_options WHERE compile_options LIKE '%FTS5%'` |
| Next.js SSR calling localhost:8000 | Fails in Docker | Use `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` env var; in Docker, use service name `http://backend:8000` |
| Stale data / dedup failures | Duplicate companies | Domain-based dedup is primary; run periodic dedup cleanup job |
| Claude returning invalid JSON | Parse errors | Retry once with explicit "return only valid JSON" instruction; fallback to regex extraction |
| Very large investor portfolios (>1000 companies) | Timeout / memory | Chunk HTML, paginate API calls, set max companies per scrape run |

input.content:
# VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator — Implementation Plan

## Context

Build a full-stack web app that scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages, uses Claude API to extract structured company data from messy HTML, and presents it through a searchable frontend. The key innovation is LLM-powered extraction instead of per-site custom parsers.

**Working directory:** `/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/`

---

## Phase 0: Scaffolding & Dependencies

### 0.1 Create directory structure
```
vc-aggregator/
├── backend/{database,scraper,api}/
└── frontend/ (via create-next-app)
```

### 0.2 Backend dependencies (`backend/requirements.txt`)
```
fastapi==0.115.6
uvicorn[standard]==0.34.0
sqlalchemy==2.0.36
aiosqlite==0.20.0
anthropic==0.46.0
playwright==1.49.1
beautifulsoup4==4.12.3
apscheduler==3.10.4
thefuzz[speedup]==0.22.1
python-levenshtein==0.26.1
httpx==0.28.1
pydantic==2.10.4
pydantic-settings==2.7.1
```
Then: `python3 -m venv venv && pip install -r requirements.txt && playwright install chromium`

### 0.3 Frontend scaffold
```bash
npx create-next-app@14 frontend --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir=no --import-alias="@/*"
cd frontend && npx shadcn-ui@latest init
npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card input table badge select dialog tabs separator skeleton switch
npm install @tanstack/react-query lucide-react
```

---

## Phase 1: Data Pipeline

### 1.1 `backend/config.py`
- `pydantic_settings.BaseSettings` loading from `.env`
- Keys: `DATABASE_URL` (default sqlite), `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` (claude-sonnet-4-20250514), `SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN/MAX`, `USER_AGENTS` list, `ENABLE_SCHEDULER`

### 1.2 `backend/database/connection.py`
- Async SQLAlchemy engine + session factory
- `init_db()`: create tables via `Base.metadata.create_all`, then raw SQL for FTS5 virtual table + sync triggers
- Enable WAL mode: `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`
- FTS5 virtual table on companies (name, description, sector columns)

### 1.3 `backend/database/models.py`
- 4 tables matching provided schema: `investors`, `companies`, `investments`, `scrape_logs`
- Add `domain` column to companies (extracted from website, used for dedup)
- Add `generate_slug()` utility
- Indexes on `companies.domain`, `companies.sector`, `companies.stage`

### 1.4 `backend/database/seed_sources.py`
- JSON seed data with 20 VCs/accelerators (as specified)
- `seed_investors()` function: upsert (skip if name exists)
- Called from `init_db()`

### 1.5 `backend/scraper/engine.py`
- `fetch_with_playwright(url)`: headless Chromium, random user-agent, block images/fonts, scroll-to-bottom for lazy loading (max 50 scrolls), return `page.content()`
- `fetch_with_httpx(url)`: simple GET fallback for static pages
- Rate limiting: `asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(2.0, 3.5))` between fetches

### 1.6 `backend/scraper/extractor.py` (CRITICAL FILE)
- `preprocess_html()`: BeautifulSoup strips scripts/styles/nav/footer/svg, keeps only href/src/alt/class attributes
- `chunk_html()`: split cleaned HTML into <=120K char chunks at natural break points
- `extract_companies()`: send each chunk to Claude API with extraction prompt, parse JSON response, merge results
- Prompt instructs Claude to return JSON array with normalized field values
- Error handling: retry once on invalid JSON, strip markdown fences

### 1.7 `backend/scraper/normalizer.py`
- `extract_domain(url)`: strip www prefix, extract clean domain
- `deduplicate_and_upsert()`:
  1. Domain match (primary dedup key)
  2. Fuzzy name match via `thefuzz` (ratio > 85)
  3. Insert new if no match
- Upsert: fill null fields from new data, don't overwrite existing
- Create `investments` junction record
- Return counts: {added, updated, skipped}

### 1.8 `backend/scraper/scheduler.py`
- APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler
- `scrape_investor(investor_id)`: full pipeline (fetch -> extract -> normalize -> store) with scrape_log recording
- Gate behind `ENABLE_SCHEDULER` env var
- Stagger jobs across the week

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** Run pipeline on 1-2 small investors (USV, First Round Capital)

---

## Phase 2: API Layer

### 2.1 `backend/api/schemas.py`
- Pydantic models: `CompanyOut`, `CompanyListOut` (paginated), `InvestorOut`, `InvestorListOut`, `ScrapeLogOut`, `StatsOut`

### 2.2 `backend/api/routes.py`
```
GET  /api/companies          — search + filter + paginate (FTS5 for text search)
GET  /api/companies/{slug}   — detail with investors
GET  /api/investors           — list (filterable by type)
GET  /api/investors/{id}      — detail with portfolio
POST /api/scrape/{investor_id} — trigger manual scrape (BackgroundTasks)
POST /api/scrape/all          — trigger all
GET  /api/scrape/logs         — recent logs
GET  /api/stats               — dashboard stats
GET  /api/filters             — available filter values
```
- FTS5 search: `JOIN companies_fts ON companies.id = companies_fts.rowid WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query`
- Filters compose with AND

### 2.3 `backend/main.py`
- FastAPI app with CORS (allow localhost:3000)
- Startup: `init_db()`, optionally `setup_scheduler()`
- Include API router

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** Start uvicorn, test all endpoints via Swagger UI

---

## Phase 3: Frontend

### 3.1 Core files
- `frontend/lib/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces matching API schemas
- `frontend/lib/api.ts` — fetch wrapper functions for all endpoints

### 3.2 Components
- `SearchBar.tsx` — debounced input, syncs with URL params
- `CompanyCard.tsx` — shadcn Card with name, description, badges, investor logos
- `FilterSidebar.tsx` — checkbox groups for sector/stage, select for investor
- `InvestorGrid.tsx` — investor card grid
- `DataTable.tsx` — shadcn Table alternative view

### 3.3 Pages
- `app/layout.tsx` — nav bar, theme provider, QueryClientProvider
- `app/page.tsx` — main search/browse: sidebar + card grid + pagination + stats banner
- `app/company/[slug]/page.tsx` — company detail with investors list
- `app/investors/page.tsx` — investor grid with type filter
- `app/investors/[id]/page.tsx` — investor detail with portfolio + scrape trigger

### Design: Clean/minimal (Linear aesthetic), shadcn/ui components, responsive, dark/light mode via next-themes

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** All 4 pages functional with real API data

---

## Phase 4: Polish

1. Activate scheduler with staggered weekly jobs
2. Scrape logs viewer (table on investors page or `/admin`)
3. Dark mode toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
4. Loading/error/empty states throughout
5. Docker: `backend/Dockerfile` (Python 3.13 + Playwright deps), `frontend/Dockerfile` (Node 22), `docker-compose.yml`
6. `README.md` with setup instructions

---

## Key Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Large HTML (YC has thousands of companies) | Aggressive HTML preprocessing + chunking; consider YC API directly |
| Claude returning invalid JSON | Strip markdown fences, retry once, log raw response |
| Sites blocking scrapers | Random user agents, 2-5s delays, respect robots.txt |
| SQLite write locks during scrape | WAL mode, scrape one investor at a time |
| FTS5 availability | Python's bundled SQLite usually includes FTS5; verify at startup |
| Dedup failures | Domain-based primary key + fuzzy name fallback |
| Cost: ~$0.50-$2 per full scrape cycle (YC outlier at $5+) | Pre-process HTML aggressively |

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Backend pipeline:** Manually scrape 2-3 investors, verify companies appear in DB with correct dedup
2. **API:** Test all endpoints via FastAPI Swagger UI at `/docs`
3. **Frontend:** Visual check all 4 pages, test search/filter/pagination, test dark mode
4. **E2E:** Search for a company known to be in multiple portfolios, verify it shows all investors
5. **Scrape trigger:** Click "Scrape" on investor detail page, verify log entry created and companies updated

---

## File Creation Order

1. `backend/requirements.txt` + venv setup
2. `backend/.env`
3. `backend/config.py`
4. `backend/database/__init__.py`, `connection.py`, `models.py`, `seed_sources.py`
5. `backend/scraper/__init__.py`, `engine.py`, `extractor.py`, `normalizer.py`, `scheduler.py`
6. Test pipeline on 2 investors
7. `backend/api/__init__.py`, `schemas.py`, `routes.py`
8. `backend/main.py`
9. Test API endpoints
10. Frontend scaffold + shadcn setup
11. `frontend/lib/types.ts`, `api.ts`
12. All components
13. All pages
14. Test E2E
15. Polish: dark mode, Docker, README

The plan covers the full build across 4 phases — data pipeline, API, frontend, and polish. Key highlights:

- **Claude-powered extraction** is the core innovation — `extractor.py` preprocesses HTML with BeautifulSoup, chunks if needed, then sends to Claude Sonnet for structured JSON extraction
- **Deduplication** uses domain matching (primary) + fuzzy name matching (fallback via thefuzz)
- **FTS5 search** requires raw SQL outside SQLAlchemy for virtual table + sync triggers
- **Cost estimate**: ~$0.50-$2 per full scrape cycle across all 20 investors
- **Test checkpoints** after each phase before moving on

The plan follows your exact project structure, database schema, API endpoints, and seed data.

## Approved Plan:
# VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator — Implementation Plan

## Context

Build a full-stack web app that scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages, uses Claude API to extract structured company data from messy HTML, and presents it through a searchable frontend. The key innovation is LLM-powered extraction instead of per-site custom parsers.

**Working directory:** `/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/`

---

## Phase 0: Scaffolding & Dependencies

### 0.1 Create directory structure
```
vc-aggregator/
├── backend/{database,scraper,api}/
└── frontend/ (via create-next-app)
```

### 0.2 Backend dependencies (`backend/requirements.txt`)
```
fastapi==0.115.6
uvicorn[standard]==0.34.0
sqlalchemy==2.0.36
aiosqlite==0.20.0
anthropic==0.46.0
playwright==1.49.1
beautifulsoup4==4.12.3
apscheduler==3.10.4
thefuzz[speedup]==0.22.1
python-levenshtein==0.26.1
httpx==0.28.1
pydantic==2.10.4
pydantic-settings==2.7.1
```
Then: `python3 -m venv venv && pip install -r requirements.txt && playwright install chromium`

### 0.3 Frontend scaffold
```bash
npx create-next-app@14 frontend --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir=no --import-alias="@/*"
cd frontend && npx shadcn-ui@latest init
npx shadcn-ui@latest add button card input table badge select dialog tabs separator skeleton switch
npm install @tanstack/react-query lucide-react
```

---

## Phase 1: Data Pipeline

### 1.1 `backend/config.py`
- `pydantic_settings.BaseSettings` loading from `.env`
- Keys: `DATABASE_URL` (default sqlite), `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_MODEL` (claude-sonnet-4-20250514), `SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN/MAX`, `USER_AGENTS` list, `ENABLE_SCHEDULER`

### 1.2 `backend/database/connection.py`
- Async SQLAlchemy engine + session factory
- `init_db()`: create tables via `Base.metadata.create_all`, then raw SQL for FTS5 virtual table + sync triggers
- Enable WAL mode: `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`
- FTS5 virtual table on companies (name, description, sector columns)

### 1.3 `backend/database/models.py`
- 4 tables matching provided schema: `investors`, `companies`, `investments`, `scrape_logs`
- Add `domain` column to companies (extracted from website, used for dedup)
- Add `generate_slug()` utility
- Indexes on `companies.domain`, `companies.sector`, `companies.stage`

### 1.4 `backend/database/seed_sources.py`
- JSON seed data with 20 VCs/accelerators (as specified)
- `seed_investors()` function: upsert (skip if name exists)
- Called from `init_db()`

### 1.5 `backend/scraper/engine.py`
- `fetch_with_playwright(url)`: headless Chromium, random user-agent, block images/fonts, scroll-to-bottom for lazy loading (max 50 scrolls), return `page.content()`
- `fetch_with_httpx(url)`: simple GET fallback for static pages
- Rate limiting: `asyncio.sleep(random.uniform(2.0, 3.5))` between fetches

### 1.6 `backend/scraper/extractor.py` (CRITICAL FILE)
- `preprocess_html()`: BeautifulSoup strips scripts/styles/nav/footer/svg, keeps only href/src/alt/class attributes
- `chunk_html()`: split cleaned HTML into <=120K char chunks at natural break points
- `extract_companies()`: send each chunk to Claude API with extraction prompt, parse JSON response, merge results
- Prompt instructs Claude to return JSON array with normalized field values
- Error handling: retry once on invalid JSON, strip markdown fences

### 1.7 `backend/scraper/normalizer.py`
- `extract_domain(url)`: strip www prefix, extract clean domain
- `deduplicate_and_upsert()`:
  1. Domain match (primary dedup key)
  2. Fuzzy name match via `thefuzz` (ratio > 85)
  3. Insert new if no match
- Upsert: fill null fields from new data, don't overwrite existing
- Create `investments` junction record
- Return counts: {added, updated, skipped}

### 1.8 `backend/scraper/scheduler.py`
- APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler
- `scrape_investor(investor_id)`: full pipeline (fetch -> extract -> normalize -> store) with scrape_log recording
- Gate behind `ENABLE_SCHEDULER` env var
- Stagger jobs across the week

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** Run pipeline on 1-2 small investors (USV, First Round Capital)

---

## Phase 2: API Layer

### 2.1 `backend/api/schemas.py`
- Pydantic models: `CompanyOut`, `CompanyListOut` (paginated), `InvestorOut`, `InvestorListOut`, `ScrapeLogOut`, `StatsOut`

### 2.2 `backend/api/routes.py`
```
GET  /api/companies          — search + filter + paginate (FTS5 for text search)
GET  /api/companies/{slug}   — detail with investors
GET  /api/investors           — list (filterable by type)
GET  /api/investors/{id}      — detail with portfolio
POST /api/scrape/{investor_id} — trigger manual scrape (BackgroundTasks)
POST /api/scrape/all          — trigger all
GET  /api/scrape/logs         — recent logs
GET  /api/stats               — dashboard stats
GET  /api/filters             — available filter values
```
- FTS5 search: `JOIN companies_fts ON companies.id = companies_fts.rowid WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query`
- Filters compose with AND

### 2.3 `backend/main.py`
- FastAPI app with CORS (allow localhost:3000)
- Startup: `init_db()`, optionally `setup_scheduler()`
- Include API router

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** Start uvicorn, test all endpoints via Swagger UI

---

## Phase 3: Frontend

### 3.1 Core files
- `frontend/lib/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces matching API schemas
- `frontend/lib/api.ts` — fetch wrapper functions for all endpoints

### 3.2 Components
- `SearchBar.tsx` — debounced input, syncs with URL params
- `CompanyCard.tsx` — shadcn Card with name, description, badges, investor logos
- `FilterSidebar.tsx` — checkbox groups for sector/stage, select for investor
- `InvestorGrid.tsx` — investor card grid
- `DataTable.tsx` — shadcn Table alternative view

### 3.3 Pages
- `app/layout.tsx` — nav bar, theme provider, QueryClientProvider
- `app/page.tsx` — main search/browse: sidebar + card grid + pagination + stats banner
- `app/company/[slug]/page.tsx` — company detail with investors list
- `app/investors/page.tsx` — investor grid with type filter
- `app/investors/[id]/page.tsx` — investor detail with portfolio + scrape trigger

### Design: Clean/minimal (Linear aesthetic), shadcn/ui components, responsive, dark/light mode via next-themes

**TEST CHECKPOINT:** All 4 pages functional with real API data

---

## Phase 4: Polish

1. Activate scheduler with staggered weekly jobs
2. Scrape logs viewer (table on investors page or `/admin`)
3. Dark mode toggle in nav (sun/moon icon)
4. Loading/error/empty states throughout
5. Docker: `backend/Dockerfile` (Python 3.13 + Playwright deps), `frontend/Dockerfile` (Node 22), `docker-compose.yml`
6. `README.md` with setup instructions

---

## Key Risks & Mitigations

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Large HTML (YC has thousands of companies) | Aggressive HTML preprocessing + chunking; consider YC API directly |
| Claude returning invalid JSON | Strip markdown fences, retry once, log raw response |
| Sites blocking scrapers | Random user agents, 2-5s delays, respect robots.txt |
| SQLite write locks during scrape | WAL mode, scrape one investor at a time |
| FTS5 availability | Python's bundled SQLite usually includes FTS5; verify at startup |
| Dedup failures | Domain-based primary key + fuzzy name fallback |
| Cost: ~$0.50-$2 per full scrape cycle (YC outlier at $5+) | Pre-process HTML aggressively |

---

## Verification Plan

1. **Backend pipeline:** Manually scrape 2-3 investors, verify companies appear in DB with correct dedup
2. **API:** Test all endpoints via FastAPI Swagger UI at `/docs`
3. **Frontend:** Visual check all 4 pages, test search/filter/pagination, test dark mode
4. **E2E:** Search for a company known to be in multiple portfolios, verify it shows all investors
5. **Scrape trigger:** Click "Scrape" on investor detail page, verify log entry created and companies updated

---

## File Creation Order

1. `backend/requirements.txt` + venv setup
2. `backend/.env`
3. `backend/config.py`
4. `backend/database/__init__.py`, `connection.py`, `models.py`, `seed_sources.py`
5. `backend/scraper/__init__.py`, `engine.py`, `extractor.py`, `normalizer.py`, `scheduler.py`
6. Test pipeline on 2 investors
7. `backend/api/__init__.py`, `schemas.py`, `routes.py`
8. `backend/main.py`
9. Test API endpoints
10. Frontend scaffold + shadcn setup
11. `frontend/lib/types.ts`, `api.ts`
12. All components
13. All pages
14. Test E2E

import re
from datetime import datetime

from sqlalchemy import (
    Boolean,
    Column,
    DateTime,
    Float,
    ForeignKey,
    Index,
    Integer,
    String,
    Text,
    UniqueConstraint,
    func,
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import DeclarativeBase, relationship


class Base(DeclarativeBase):
    pass


class Investor(Base):
    __tablename__ = "investors"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False, unique=True)
    type = Column(String, nullable=False)  # vc | accelerator | angel | corporate
    website = Column(String, nullable=True)
    portfolio_url = Column(String, nullable=False)
    logo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    location = Column(String, nullable=True)
    last_scraped_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    scrape_status = Column(String, default="pending")  # pending | success | failed
    created_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())

    investments = relationship("Investment", back_populates="investor")
    scrape_logs = relationship("ScrapeLog", back_populates="investor")


class Company(Base):
    __tablename__ = "companies"
    __table_args__ = (
        Index("ix_companies_domain", "domain"),
        Index("ix_companies_sector", "sector"),
        Index("ix_companies_stage", "stage"),
    )

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    name = Column(String, nullable=False)
    slug = Column(String, unique=True, nullable=False)
    website = Column(String, nullable=True)
    domain = Column(String, nullable=True)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    long_description = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    sector = Column(String, nullable=True)
    stage = Column(String, nullable=True)
    founded_year = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    location = Column(String, nullable=True)
    employee_count = Column(String, nullable=True)
    logo_url = Column(String, nullable=True)
    status = Column(String, default="active")  # active | acquired | dead | ipo
    created_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now(), onupdate=func.now())

    investments = relationship("Investment", back_populates="company")


class Investment(Base):
    __tablename__ = "investments"
    __table_args__ = (
        UniqueConstraint("investor_id", "company_id", "round_type", name="uq_investment"),
    )

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    investor_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("investors.id"), nullable=False)
    company_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("companies.id"), nullable=False)
    investment_date = Column(String, nullable=True)
    round_type = Column(String, nullable=True)
    amount = Column(String, nullable=True)
    is_lead = Column(Boolean, default=False)
    source_url = Column(String, nullable=True)
    first_seen_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now())
    last_confirmed_at = Column(DateTime, server_default=func.now())

    investor = relationship("Investor", back_populates="investments")
    company = relationship("Company", back_populates="investments")


class ScrapeLog(Base):
    __tablename__ = "scrape_logs"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    investor_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("investors.id"), nullable=False)
    started_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    finished_at = Column(DateTime, nullable=True)
    status = Column(String, nullable=True)  # success | failed | partial
    companies_found = Column(Integer, default=0)
    companies_new = Column(Integer, default=0)
    companies_updated = Column(Integer, default=0)
    error_message = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    duration_seconds = Column(Float, nullable=True)

    investor = relationship("Investor", back_populates="scrape_logs")


def generate_slug(name: str) -> str:
    """Generate a URL-friendly slug from a company name."""
    slug = name.lower().strip()
    slug = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", slug)
    slug = re.sub(r"[\s_]+", "-", slug)
    slug = re.sub(r"-+", "-", slug)
    slug = slug.strip("-")
    return slug
