input.content:
import logging

from sqlalchemy import event, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession, async_sessionmaker, create_async_engine

from config import settings
from database.models import Base

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

engine = create_async_engine(settings.DATABASE_URL, echo=False)
async_session = async_sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)


def _set_sqlite_pragmas(dbapi_conn, connection_record):
    """Enable WAL mode and foreign keys for SQLite."""
    cursor = dbapi_conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    cursor.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON")
    cursor.close()


event.listens_for(engine.sync_engine, "connect")(_set_sqlite_pragmas)


async def init_db():
    """Create all tables, FTS5 virtual table, and seed data."""
    async with engine.begin() as conn:
        await conn.run_sync(Base.metadata.create_all)

        # Create FTS5 virtual table for full-text search on companies
        await conn.execute(text("""
            CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS companies_fts USING fts5(
                name,
                description,
                sector,
                content=companies,
                content_rowid=id
            )
        """))

        # Triggers to keep FTS index in sync
        await conn.execute(text("""
            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ai AFTER INSERT ON companies BEGIN
                INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, sector)
                VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.sector);
            END
        """))

        await conn.execute(text("""
            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_ad AFTER DELETE ON companies BEGIN
                INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, sector)
                VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.sector);
            END
        """))

        await conn.execute(text("""
            CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS companies_au AFTER UPDATE ON companies BEGIN
                INSERT INTO companies_fts(companies_fts, rowid, name, description, sector)
                VALUES('delete', old.id, old.name, old.description, old.sector);
                INSERT INTO companies_fts(rowid, name, description, sector)
                VALUES (new.id, new.name, new.description, new.sector);
            END
        """))

    # Seed investors
    from database.seed_sources import seed_investors

    async with async_session() as session:
        await seed_investors(session)

    logger.info("Database initialized successfully")


async def get_db():
    """FastAPI dependency for database sessions."""
    async with async_session() as session:
        yield session

import logging

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from database.models import Investor

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SEED_INVESTORS = [
    {"name": "Y Combinator", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.ycombinator.com/companies", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Techstars", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.techstars.com/portfolio", "location": "Boulder, CO"},
    {"name": "500 Global", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://500.co/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Sequoia Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.sequoiacap.com/our-companies/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "a16z", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://a16z.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Accel", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.accel.com/portfolio", "location": "Palo Alto, CA"},
    {"name": "Benchmark", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.benchmark.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "First Round Capital", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://firstround.com/companies/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Greylock", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://greylock.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Lightspeed Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://lsvp.com/portfolio/", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Founders Fund", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://foundersfund.com/portfolio/", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "Bessemer Venture Partners", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.bvp.com/portfolio", "location": "San Francisco, CA"},
    {"name": "NEA", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.nea.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Index Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.indexventures.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},
    {"name": "GV (Google Ventures)", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.gv.com/portfolio/", "location": "Mountain View, CA"},
    {"name": "Kleiner Perkins", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.kleinerperkins.com/portfolio", "location": "Menlo Park, CA"},
    {"name": "Union Square Ventures", "type": "vc", "portfolio_url": "https://www.usv.com/portfolio/", "location": "New York, NY"},
    {"name": "Antler", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.antler.co/portfolio", "location": "Singapore"},
    {"name": "Entrepreneur First", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.joinef.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},
    {"name": "Plug and Play", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.plugandplaytechcenter.com/portfolio/", "location": "Sunnyvale, CA"},
]


async def seed_investors(session: AsyncSession):
    """Insert seed investors if they don't already exist."""
    existing = await session.execute(select(Investor.name))
    existing_names = {row[0] for row in existing.fetchall()}

    added = 0
    for data in SEED_INVESTORS:
        if data["name"] not in existing_names:
            investor = Investor(**data)
            session.add(investor)
            added += 1

    if added > 0:
        await session.commit()
        logger.info(f"Seeded {added} investors")
    else:
        logger.info("All investors already seeded")

    {"name": "Entrepreneur First", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.joinef.com/companies/", "location": "London, UK"},
    {"name": "Plug and Play", "type": "accelerator", "portfolio_url": "https://www.plugandplaytechcenter.com/portfolio/", "location": "Sunnyvale, CA"},
]


async def seed_investors(session: AsyncSession):
    """Insert seed investors if they don't already exist."""
    existing = await session.execute(select(Investor.name))
    existing_names = {row[0] for row in existing.fetchall()}

    added = 0
    for data in SEED_INVESTORS:
        if data["name"] not in existing_names:
            investor = Investor(**data)
            session.add(investor)
            added += 1

    if added > 0:
        await session.commit()
        logger.info(f"Seeded {added} investors")
    else:
        logger.info("All investors already seeded")

import asyncio
import logging
import random

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

from config import settings

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def fetch_with_playwright(
    url: str,
    scroll: bool = True,
    max_scrolls: int = 50,
    wait_after_scroll_ms: int = 1500,
) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using headless Chromium, handling JS rendering and lazy loading."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with Playwright: {url}")

    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(
            user_agent=random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS),
            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
        )
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Block heavy resources to speed up loading
        await page.route(
            "**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,svg,woff,woff2,ttf,eot,mp4,webm,webp,ico}",
            lambda route: route.abort(),
        )

        try:
            await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=60000)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Navigation timeout/error for {url}: {e}")
            # Still try to get whatever loaded
            pass

        if scroll:
            prev_height = 0
            for i in range(max_scrolls):
                curr_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight")
                if curr_height == prev_height:
                    break
                await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
                await page.wait_for_timeout(wait_after_scroll_ms)
                prev_height = curr_height
                if i > 0 and i % 10 == 0:
                    logger.info(f"Scrolled {i} times, page height: {curr_height}")

        html = await page.content()
        await browser.close()

        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(html)} chars from {url}")
        return html


async def fetch_with_httpx(url: str) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using plain HTTP (for static pages)."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with httpx: {url}")

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(
        headers={"User-Agent": random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS)},
        follow_redirects=True,
        timeout=30.0,
    ) as client:
        response = await client.get(url)
        response.raise_for_status()
        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(response.text)} chars from {url}")
        return response.text


def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
    """Strip unnecessary HTML elements to reduce token count before sending to Claude."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, "html.parser")

    # Remove non-content elements
    for tag in soup(
        ["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript",
         "meta", "link", "iframe", "video", "audio", "source", "picture"]
    ):
        tag.decompose()

    # Strip most attributes, keep only structurally useful ones
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        allowed = {}
        for attr in ("href", "src", "alt", "class", "data-company", "data-name"):
            val = tag.get(attr)
            if val:
                # Truncate long class lists
                if attr == "class" and isinstance(val, list):
                    val = " ".join(val[:3])
                allowed[attr] = val
        tag.attrs = allowed

    # Remove empty tags (except br, hr, img)
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        if tag.name not in ("br", "hr", "img") and not tag.get_text(strip=True) and not tag.find("img"):
            tag.decompose()

    cleaned = str(soup)
    logger.info(f"Preprocessed HTML: {len(raw_html)} -> {len(cleaned)} chars")
    return cleaned


def chunk_html(html: str, max_chars: int | None = None) -> list[str]:
    """Split HTML into chunks if it exceeds max_chars."""
    if max_chars is None:
        max_chars = settings.MAX_HTML_CHARS

    if len(html) <= max_chars:
        return [html]

    chunks = []
    # Try to split at div boundaries
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.find("body") or soup

    current_chunk = ""
    for child in body.children:
        child_str = str(child)
        if len(current_chunk) + len(child_str) > max_chars and current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk)
            current_chunk = child_str
        else:
            current_chunk += child_str

    if current_chunk:
        chunks.append(current_chunk)

    # If we couldn't split meaningfully, fall back to character splits
    if not chunks:
        for i in range(0, len(html), max_chars):
            chunks.append(html[i : i + max_chars])

    logger.info(f"Split HTML into {len(chunks)} chunks")
    return chunks


async def rate_limit_delay():
    """Random delay between scraping requests."""
    delay = random.uniform(settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN, settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MAX)
    await asyncio.sleep(delay)

import json
import logging
import re

import anthropic

from config import settings
from scraper.engine import chunk_html, preprocess_html

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

EXTRACTION_PROMPT = """You are a data extraction engine. You will receive HTML from a venture capital or accelerator portfolio page.

Extract ALL companies/startups listed on this page into a JSON array.

For each company, extract whatever fields are available:
- name (required)
- website_url
- description (short, one-liner if available)
- sector (normalize to lowercase: "fintech", "healthtech", "saas", "ai", "devtools", "biotech", "edtech", "climate", "crypto", "ecommerce", "marketplace", "cybersecurity", "enterprise", "consumer", "infrastructure", "other")
- stage (normalize to: "pre-seed", "seed", "series-a", "series-b", "series-c", "series-d", "growth", "ipo")
- location
- founded_year
- logo_url
- status ("active", "acquired", "dead", "ipo" — default to "active" if not specified)

Rules:
- Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown formatting, no explanation, no code fences
- If a field is not available, omit it (don't include null values)
- Extract ALL companies, not just featured ones
- If the page has hundreds of companies, extract every single one

Response format:
[
  {"name": "CompanyName", "website_url": "https://...", "description": "...", "sector": "...", ...},
  ...
]"""


async def extract_companies(html: str, investor_name: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Use Claude API to extract structured company data from portfolio page HTML."""
    cleaned = preprocess_html(html)
    chunks = chunk_html(cleaned)

    client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=settings.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
    all_companies = []

    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        logger.info(
            f"Extracting from chunk {i + 1}/{len(chunks)} "
            f"({len(chunk)} chars) for {investor_name}"
        )

        user_message = (
            f"Extract all companies from this {investor_name} portfolio page HTML"
        )
        if len(chunks) > 1:
            user_message += f" (part {i + 1} of {len(chunks)})"
        user_message += f":\n\n{chunk}"

        try:
            response = await client.messages.create(
                model=settings.ANTHROPIC_MODEL,
                max_tokens=8192,
                system=EXTRACTION_PROMPT,
                messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_message}],
            )

            raw_text = response.content[0].text
            companies = _parse_json_response(raw_text)
            all_companies.extend(companies)

            logger.info(
                f"Extracted {len(companies)} companies from chunk {i + 1}"
            )

        except anthropic.APIError as e:
            logger.error(f"Claude API error for {investor_name} chunk {i + 1}: {e}")
            continue
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(
                f"Extraction error for {investor_name} chunk {i + 1}: {e}"
            )
            continue

    logger.info(f"Total extracted for {investor_name}: {len(all_companies)} companies")
    return all_companies


def _parse_json_response(text: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Parse Claude's JSON response, handling common formatting issues."""
    # Strip markdown code fences if present
    text = text.strip()
    text = re.sub(r"^```(?:json)?\s*", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"\s*```$", "", text)
    text = text.strip()

    try:
        result = json.loads(text)
        if isinstance(result, list):
            return result
        if isinstance(result, dict) and "companies" in result:
            return result["companies"]
        logger.warning(f"Unexpected JSON structure: {type(result)}")
        return []
    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
        logger.error(f"JSON parse error: {e}")
        logger.debug(f"Raw response (first 500 chars): {text[:500]}")

        # Try to find JSON array in the text
        match = re.search(r"\[.*\]", text, re.DOTALL)
        if match:
            try:
                return json.loads(match.group())
            except json.JSONDecodeError:
                pass

        return []

input.content:
import logging
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from thefuzz import fuzz

from database.models import Company, Investment, generate_slug

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 85


def extract_domain(url: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Extract clean domain from a URL. https://www.stripe.com/pricing -> stripe.com"""
    if not url:
        return None
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}")
        domain = parsed.netloc.lower()
        if domain.startswith("www."):
            domain = domain[4:]
        return domain or None
    except Exception:
        return None


def normalize_sector(sector: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Normalize sector names to consistent lowercase values."""
    if not sector:
        return None

    sector = sector.lower().strip()

    mapping = {
        "artificial intelligence": "ai",
        "machine learning": "ai",
        "ai/ml": "ai",
        "financial technology": "fintech",
        "financial services": "fintech",
        "health": "healthtech",
        "healthcare": "healthtech",
        "health tech": "healthtech",
        "software as a service": "saas",
        "software": "saas",
        "developer tools": "devtools",
        "dev tools": "devtools",
        "developer": "devtools",
        "education": "edtech",
        "education technology": "edtech",
        "climate tech": "climate",
        "clean tech": "climate",
        "cleantech": "climate",
        "energy": "climate",
        "sustainability": "climate",
        "blockchain": "crypto",
        "web3": "crypto",
        "cryptocurrency": "crypto",
        "e-commerce": "ecommerce",
        "commerce": "ecommerce",
        "retail": "ecommerce",
        "security": "cybersecurity",
        "cyber security": "cybersecurity",
        "information security": "cybersecurity",
        "biotechnology": "biotech",
        "life sciences": "biotech",
        "bio": "biotech",
    }

    return mapping.get(sector, sector)


def normalize_stage(stage: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Normalize funding stage names."""
    if not stage:
        return None

    stage = stage.lower().strip()

    mapping = {
        "pre seed": "pre-seed",
        "preseed": "pre-seed",
        "angel": "pre-seed",
        "series a": "series-a",
        "series b": "series-b",
        "series c": "series-c",
        "series d": "series-d",
        "series e": "growth",
        "series f": "growth",
        "late stage": "growth",
        "late-stage": "growth",
        "public": "ipo",
        "went public": "ipo",
    }

    return mapping.get(stage, stage)


async def find_existing_company(
    session: AsyncSession, name: str, domain: str | None
) -> Company | None:
    """Find an existing company by domain match or fuzzy name match."""
    # 1. Domain match (most reliable)
    if domain:
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Company).where(Company.domain == domain)
        )
        match = result.scalar_one_or_none()
        if match:
            return match

    # 2. Fuzzy name match
    result = await session.execute(select(Company))
    all_companies = result.scalars().all()

    for company in all_companies:
        ratio = fuzz.token_sort_ratio(name.lower(), company.name.lower())
        if ratio >= FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD:
            logger.debug(f"Fuzzy matched '{name}' -> '{company.name}' (ratio={ratio})")
            return company

    return None


async def ensure_unique_slug(session: AsyncSession, base_slug: str) -> str:
    """Generate a unique slug, appending -2, -3, etc. if needed."""
    slug = base_slug
    counter = 2
    while True:
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Company).where(Company.slug == slug)
        )
        if result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
            return slug
        slug = f"{base_slug}-{counter}"
        counter += 1


def _update_if_empty(company: Company, field: str, value) -> bool:
    """Update a company field only if it's currently empty."""
    if value and not getattr(company, field):
        setattr(company, field, value)
        return True
    return False


async def deduplicate_and_upsert(
    session: AsyncSession,
    companies_data: list[dict],
    investor_id: int,
    source_url: str,
) -> dict:
    """Process extracted companies: deduplicate, upsert, and create investment links."""
    stats = {"added": 0, "updated": 0, "skipped": 0, "linked": 0}

    for data in companies_data:
        name = data.get("name", "").strip()
        if not name:
            stats["skipped"] += 1
            continue

        website = data.get("website_url") or data.get("website")
        domain = extract_domain(website)
        sector = normalize_sector(data.get("sector") or data.get("industry"))
        stage = normalize_stage(data.get("stage"))

        # Try to find existing company
        existing = await find_existing_company(session, name, domain)

        if existing:
            # Update empty fields with new data
            updated = False
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "website", website)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "domain", domain)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "description", data.get("description"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "sector", sector)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "stage", stage)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "founded_year", data.get("founded_year"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "location", data.get("location"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "logo_url", data.get("logo_url"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "status", data.get("status"))

            if updated:
                stats["updated"] += 1

            company = existing
        else:
            # Create new company
            slug = generate_slug(name)
            slug = await ensure_unique_slug(session, slug)

            company = Company(
                name=name,
                slug=slug,
                website=website,
                domain=domain,
                description=data.get("description"),
                sector=sector,
                stage=stage,
                founded_year=data.get("founded_year"),
                location=data.get("location"),
                logo_url=data.get("logo_url"),
                status=data.get("status", "active"),
            )
            session.add(company)
            await session.flush()  # Get the ID
            stats["added"] += 1

        # Create investment link if it doesn't exist
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Investment).where(
                Investment.investor_id == investor_id,
                Investment.company_id == company.id,
            )
        )
        if result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
            investment = Investment(
                investor_id=investor_id,
                company_id=company.id,
                round_type=stage,
                source_url=source_url,
            )
            session.add(investment)
            stats["linked"] += 1

    await session.commit()
    logger.info(
        f"Upsert results: {stats['added']} added, {stats['updated']} updated, "
        f"{stats['linked']} linked, {stats['skipped']} skipped"
    )
    return stats

import logging
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from sqlalchemy import select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from thefuzz import fuzz

from database.models import Company, Investment, generate_slug

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 85


def extract_domain(url: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Extract clean domain from a URL. https://www.stripe.com/pricing -> stripe.com"""
    if not url:
        return None
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url if "://" in url else f"https://{url}")
        domain = parsed.netloc.lower()
        if domain.startswith("www."):
            domain = domain[4:]
        return domain or None
    except Exception:
        return None


def normalize_sector(sector: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Normalize sector names to consistent lowercase values."""
    if not sector:
        return None

    sector = sector.lower().strip()

    mapping = {
        "artificial intelligence": "ai",
        "machine learning": "ai",
        "ai/ml": "ai",
        "financial technology": "fintech",
        "financial services": "fintech",
        "health": "healthtech",
        "healthcare": "healthtech",
        "health tech": "healthtech",
        "software as a service": "saas",
        "software": "saas",
        "developer tools": "devtools",
        "dev tools": "devtools",
        "developer": "devtools",
        "education": "edtech",
        "education technology": "edtech",
        "climate tech": "climate",
        "clean tech": "climate",
        "cleantech": "climate",
        "energy": "climate",
        "sustainability": "climate",
        "blockchain": "crypto",
        "web3": "crypto",
        "cryptocurrency": "crypto",
        "e-commerce": "ecommerce",
        "commerce": "ecommerce",
        "retail": "ecommerce",
        "security": "cybersecurity",
        "cyber security": "cybersecurity",
        "information security": "cybersecurity",
        "biotechnology": "biotech",
        "life sciences": "biotech",
        "bio": "biotech",
    }

    return mapping.get(sector, sector)


def normalize_stage(stage: str | None) -> str | None:
    """Normalize funding stage names."""
    if not stage:
        return None

    stage = stage.lower().strip()

    mapping = {
        "pre seed": "pre-seed",
        "preseed": "pre-seed",
        "angel": "pre-seed",
        "series a": "series-a",
        "series b": "series-b",
        "series c": "series-c",
        "series d": "series-d",
        "series e": "growth",
        "series f": "growth",
        "late stage": "growth",
        "late-stage": "growth",
        "public": "ipo",
        "went public": "ipo",
    }

    return mapping.get(stage, stage)


async def find_existing_company(
    session: AsyncSession, name: str, domain: str | None
) -> Company | None:
    """Find an existing company by domain match or fuzzy name match."""
    # 1. Domain match (most reliable)
    if domain:
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Company).where(Company.domain == domain)
        )
        match = result.scalar_one_or_none()
        if match:
            return match

    # 2. Fuzzy name match
    result = await session.execute(select(Company))
    all_companies = result.scalars().all()

    for company in all_companies:
        ratio = fuzz.token_sort_ratio(name.lower(), company.name.lower())
        if ratio >= FUZZY_MATCH_THRESHOLD:
            logger.debug(f"Fuzzy matched '{name}' -> '{company.name}' (ratio={ratio})")
            return company

    return None


async def ensure_unique_slug(session: AsyncSession, base_slug: str) -> str:
    """Generate a unique slug, appending -2, -3, etc. if needed."""
    slug = base_slug
    counter = 2
    while True:
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Company).where(Company.slug == slug)
        )
        if result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
            return slug
        slug = f"{base_slug}-{counter}"
        counter += 1


def _update_if_empty(company: Company, field: str, value) -> bool:
    """Update a company field only if it's currently empty."""
    if value and not getattr(company, field):
        setattr(company, field, value)
        return True
    return False


async def deduplicate_and_upsert(
    session: AsyncSession,
    companies_data: list[dict],
    investor_id: int,
    source_url: str,
) -> dict:
    """Process extracted companies: deduplicate, upsert, and create investment links."""
    stats = {"added": 0, "updated": 0, "skipped": 0, "linked": 0}

    for data in companies_data:
        name = data.get("name", "").strip()
        if not name:
            stats["skipped"] += 1
            continue

        website = data.get("website_url") or data.get("website")
        domain = extract_domain(website)
        sector = normalize_sector(data.get("sector") or data.get("industry"))
        stage = normalize_stage(data.get("stage"))

        # Try to find existing company
        existing = await find_existing_company(session, name, domain)

        if existing:
            # Update empty fields with new data
            updated = False
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "website", website)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "domain", domain)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "description", data.get("description"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "sector", sector)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "stage", stage)
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "founded_year", data.get("founded_year"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "location", data.get("location"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "logo_url", data.get("logo_url"))
            updated |= _update_if_empty(existing, "status", data.get("status"))

            if updated:
                stats["updated"] += 1

            company = existing
        else:
            # Create new company
            slug = generate_slug(name)
            slug = await ensure_unique_slug(session, slug)

            company = Company(
                name=name,
                slug=slug,
                website=website,
                domain=domain,
                description=data.get("description"),
                sector=sector,
                stage=stage,
                founded_year=data.get("founded_year"),
                location=data.get("location"),
                logo_url=data.get("logo_url"),
                status=data.get("status", "active"),
            )
            session.add(company)
            await session.flush()  # Get the ID
            stats["added"] += 1

        # Create investment link if it doesn't exist
        result = await session.execute(
            select(Investment).where(
                Investment.investor_id == investor_id,
                Investment.company_id == company.id,
            )
        )
        if result.scalar_one_or_none() is None:
            investment = Investment(
                investor_id=investor_id,
                company_id=company.id,
                round_type=stage,
                source_url=source_url,
            )
            session.add(investment)
            stats["linked"] += 1

    await session.commit()
    logger.info(
        f"Upsert results: {stats['added']} added, {stats['updated']} updated, "
        f"{stats['linked']} linked, {stats['skipped']} skipped"
    )
    return stats

import logging
from datetime import datetime

from sqlalchemy import select

from config import settings
from database.connection import async_session
from database.models import Investor, ScrapeLog
from scraper.engine import fetch_with_playwright, rate_limit_delay
from scraper.extractor import extract_companies
from scraper.normalizer import deduplicate_and_upsert

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def scrape_investor(investor_id: int) -> dict:
    """Full scraping pipeline for a single investor."""
    async with async_session() as session:
        investor = await session.get(Investor, investor_id)
        if not investor:
            raise ValueError(f"Investor {investor_id} not found")

        log = ScrapeLog(
            investor_id=investor_id,
            started_at=datetime.utcnow(),
            status="running",
        )
        session.add(log)
        await session.commit()

        try:
            logger.info(f"Starting scrape for {investor.name}: {investor.portfolio_url}")

            # Step 1: Fetch the portfolio page
            html = await fetch_with_playwright(investor.portfolio_url)

            # Step 2: Extract companies using Claude
            companies = await extract_companies(html, investor.name)

            # Step 3: Deduplicate and store
            result = await deduplicate_and_upsert(
                session, companies, investor_id, investor.portfolio_url
            )

            log.status = "success"
            log.companies_found = len(companies)
            log.companies_new = result["added"]
            log.companies_updated = result["updated"]

            investor.last_scraped_at = datetime.utcnow()
            investor.scrape_status = "success"

            logger.info(
                f"Scrape complete for {investor.name}: "
                f"{len(companies)} found, {result['added']} new, {result['updated']} updated"
            )

        except Exception as e:
            log.status = "failed"
            log.error_message = str(e)[:2000]
            investor.scrape_status = "failed"
            logger.error(f"Scrape failed for {investor.name}: {e}")

        finally:
            log.finished_at = datetime.utcnow()
            if log.started_at and log.finished_at:
                log.duration_seconds = (
                    log.finished_at - log.started_at
                ).total_seconds()
            await session.commit()

        return {
            "investor": investor.name,
            "status": log.status,
            "companies_found": log.companies_found,
            "companies_new": log.companies_new,
            "companies_updated": log.companies_updated,
            "error": log.error_message,
        }


async def scrape_all_investors():
    """Scrape all active investors with rate limiting between each."""
    async with async_session() as session:
        result = await session.execute(select(Investor))
        investors = result.scalars().all()

    logger.info(f"Starting full scrape cycle for {len(investors)} investors")
    results = []

    for investor in investors:
        try:
            result = await scrape_investor(investor.id)
            results.append(result)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to scrape {investor.name}: {e}")
            results.append({"investor": investor.name, "status": "failed", "error": str(e)})

        await rate_limit_delay()

    successes = sum(1 for r in results if r.get("status") == "success")
    logger.info(f"Full scrape cycle complete: {successes}/{len(results)} succeeded")
    return results


def setup_scheduler():
    """Set up APScheduler for periodic scraping."""
    if not settings.ENABLE_SCHEDULER:
        logger.info("Scheduler disabled via ENABLE_SCHEDULER setting")
        return None

    from apscheduler.schedulers.asyncio import AsyncIOScheduler

    scheduler = AsyncIOScheduler()
    # Run full scrape weekly on Sundays at 2 AM
    scheduler.add_job(
        scrape_all_investors,
        "cron",
        day_of_week="sun",
        hour=2,
        minute=0,
        id="weekly_scrape",
        replace_existing=True,
    )
    scheduler.start()
    logger.info("Scheduler started — weekly scrape set for Sundays at 2 AM")
    return scheduler

from datetime import datetime

from pydantic import BaseModel


# --- Investor schemas ---

class InvestorBrief(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    type: str
    logo_url: str | None = None

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class InvestorOut(BaseModel):
    id: int
    name: str
    type: str
    website: str | None = None
    portfolio_url: str
    logo_url: str | None = None
    description: str | None = None
    location: str | None = None
    last_scraped_at: datetime | None = None
    scrape_status: str | None = None
    portfolio_count: int = 0

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class InvestorListOut(BaseModel):
    items: list[InvestorOut]
    total: int


# --- Company schemas ---

class CompanyOut(BaseModel):
    id: int

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}


class CompanyListOut(BaseModel):
    items: list[CompanyOut]
    total: int

    page_size: int

# --- Scrape log schemas ---

    companies_updated: int = 0

    model_config = {"from_attributes": True}

class StatsOut(BaseModel):

# --- Filter values ---

class FiltersOut(BaseModel):
    sectors: list[str]
    stages: list[str]
    investors: list[InvestorBrief]

import math

from fastapi import APIRouter, BackgroundTasks, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from sqlalchemy import func, select, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload

from api.schemas import (
    CompanyListOut,
    CompanyOut,
    FiltersOut,
    InvestorBrief,
    InvestorListOut,
    InvestorOut,
    ScrapeLogOut,
    StatsOut,
)
from database.connection import get_db
from database.models import Company, Investment, Investor, ScrapeLog
from scraper.scheduler import scrape_all_investors, scrape_investor

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api")


# --- Companies ---


@router.get("/companies", response_model=CompanyListOut)
async def list_companies(
    q: str | None = None,
    sector: str | None = None,
    stage: str | None = None,
    investor: str | None = None,
    investor_id: int | None = None,
    location: str | None = None,
    sort: str = "newest",
    page: int = Query(1, ge=1),
    per_page: int = Query(24, ge=1, le=100),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    """List/search companies with filters and pagination."""

    if q:
        # FTS5 search
        fts_query = text(
            "SELECT rowid FROM companies_fts WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query ORDER BY rank"
        )
        fts_result = await db.execute(fts_query, {"query": q})
        matching_ids = [row[0] for row in fts_result.fetchall()]

        if not matching_ids:
            return CompanyListOut(
                items=[], total=0, page=page, page_size=per_page, total_pages=0
            )

        query = select(Company).where(Company.id.in_(matching_ids))
    else:
        query = select(Company)

    # Apply filters
    if sector:
        sectors = [s.strip().lower() for s in sector.split(",")]
        query = query.where(Company.sector.in_(sectors))

    if stage:
        stages = [s.strip().lower() for s in stage.split(",")]
        query = query.where(Company.stage.in_(stages))

    if location:
        query = query.where(Company.location.ilike(f"%{location}%"))

    if investor:
        query = query.join(Investment).join(Investor).where(
            Investor.name.ilike(f"%{investor}%")
        )

    if investor_id:
        query = query.join(Investment).where(
            Investment.investor_id == investor_id
        )

    # Count total
    count_query = select(func.count()).select_from(query.subquery())
    total = (await db.execute(count_query)).scalar() or 0

    # Sort
    if sort == "name":
        query = query.order_by(Company.name)
    elif sort == "oldest":
        query = query.order_by(Company.created_at.asc())
    else:  # newest
        query = query.order_by(Company.created_at.desc())

    # Paginate
    offset = (page - 1) * per_page
    query = query.offset(offset).limit(per_page)

    result = await db.execute(query)
    companies = result.scalars().all()

    # Load investors for each company
    items = []
    for company in companies:
        investments = await db.execute(
            select(Investment)
            .where(Investment.company_id == company.id)
            .options(selectinload(Investment.investor))
        )
        inv_list = investments.scalars().all()

        company_dict = CompanyOut(
            id=company.id,
            name=company.name,

import math

from fastapi import APIRouter, BackgroundTasks, Depends, HTTPException, Query
from sqlalchemy import func, select, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload

from api.schemas import (
    CompanyListOut,
    CompanyOut,
    FiltersOut,
    InvestorBrief,
    InvestorListOut,
    InvestorOut,
    ScrapeLogOut,
    StatsOut,
)
from database.connection import get_db
from database.models import Company, Investment, Investor, ScrapeLog
from scraper.scheduler import scrape_all_investors, scrape_investor

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api")


# --- Companies ---


@router.get("/companies", response_model=CompanyListOut)
async def list_companies(
    q: str | None = None,
    sector: str | None = None,
    stage: str | None = None,
    investor: str | None = None,
    investor_id: int | None = None,
    location: str | None = None,
    sort: str = "newest",
    page: int = Query(1, ge=1),
    per_page: int = Query(24, ge=1, le=100),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    """List/search companies with filters and pagination."""

    if q:
        # FTS5 search
        fts_query = text(
            "SELECT rowid FROM companies_fts WHERE companies_fts MATCH :query ORDER BY rank"
        )
        fts_result = await db.execute(fts_query, {"query": q})
        matching_ids = [row[0] for row in fts_result.fetchall()]

        if not matching_ids:
            return CompanyListOut(
                items=[], total=0, page=page, page_size=per_page, total_pages=0
            )

        query = select(Company).where(Company.id.in_(matching_ids))
    else:
        query = select(Company)

    # Apply filters
    if sector:
        sectors = [s.strip().lower() for s in sector.split(",")]
        query = query.where(Company.sector.in_(sectors))

    if stage:
        stages = [s.strip().lower() for s in stage.split(",")]
        query = query.where(Company.stage.in_(stages))

    if location:
        query = query.where(Company.location.ilike(f"%{location}%"))

    if investor:
        query = query.join(Investment).join(Investor).where(
            Investor.name.ilike(f"%{investor}%")
        )

    if investor_id:
        query = query.join(Investment).where(
            Investment.investor_id == investor_id
        )

    # Count total
    count_query = select(func.count()).select_from(query.subquery())
    total = (await db.execute(count_query)).scalar() or 0

    # Sort
    if sort == "name":
        query = query.order_by(Company.name)
    elif sort == "oldest":
        query = query.order_by(Company.created_at.asc())
    else:  # newest
        query = query.order_by(Company.created_at.desc())

    # Paginate
    offset = (page - 1) * per_page
    query = query.offset(offset).limit(per_page)

    result = await db.execute(query)
    companies = result.scalars().all()

    # Load investors for each company
    items = []
    for company in companies:
        investments = await db.execute(
            select(Investment)
            .where(Investment.company_id == company.id)
            .options(selectinload(Investment.investor))
        )
        inv_list = investments.scalars().all()

        company_dict = CompanyOut(
            id=company.id,
            name=company.name,
            slug=company.slug,
            website=company.website,
            description=company.description,
            long_description=company.long_description,
            sector=company.sector,
            stage=company.stage,
            founded_year=company.founded_year,
            location=company.location,
            employee_count=company.employee_count,
            logo_url=company.logo_url,
            status=company.status,
            investors=[
                InvestorBrief(
                    id=inv.investor.id,
                    name=inv.investor.name,
                    type=inv.investor.type,
                    logo_url=inv.investor.logo_url,
                )
                for inv in inv_list
                if inv.investor
            ],
        )
        items.append(company_dict)

    total_pages = math.ceil(total / per_page) if total > 0 else 0

    return CompanyListOut(
        items=items,
        total=total,
        page=page,
        page_size=per_page,
        total_pages=total_pages,
    )


@router.get("/companies/{slug}", response_model=CompanyOut)
async def get_company(slug: str, db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db)):

    result = await db.execute(select(Company).where(Company.slug == slug))

import logging

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

from api.routes import router
from config import settings
from database.connection import init_db
from scraper.scheduler import setup_scheduler

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
)

app = FastAPI(
    title="VC Portfolio Aggregator",
    description="Scrapes, normalizes, and aggregates startup portfolio data from VC firms and accelerators",
    version="1.0.0",
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=[
        "http://localhost:3000",
        "http://127.0.0.1:3000",
    ],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(router)


@app.on_event("startup")
async def startup():
    await init_db()
    if settings.ENABLE_SCHEDULER:
        setup_scheduler()


@app.get("/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}

    async with engine.begin() as conn:

        print('Tables:', tables)

asyncio.run(test())

FROM python:3.13-slim

# Install system dependencies for Playwright
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libnss3 libatk1.0-0 libatk-bridge2.0-0 libcups2 libdrm2 \
    libxkbcommon0 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxrandr2 libgbm1 \
    libpango-1.0-0 libasound2 libxshmfence1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
RUN playwright install chromium

COPY . .

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

FROM node:22-alpine AS base

FROM base AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

services:

    volumes:

    ports:

    environment:
      - NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:8000
    depends_on:

input.content:
# VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator

Scrapes, normalizes, and aggregates startup portfolio data from VC firms and accelerators into a single searchable database. Uses Claude API to extract structured data from messy HTML — no custom parser per site needed.

## Tech Stack

- **Backend:** Python, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, SQLite (FTS5), Playwright, BeautifulSoup
- **AI Extraction:** Claude API (Anthropic SDK) for HTML → structured data
- **Frontend:** Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query
- **Scheduling:** APScheduler for periodic re-scraping

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.11+
- Node.js 18+
- An [Anthropic API key](https://console.anthropic.com/)

### Backend

```bash
cd backend
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
playwright install chromium

# Configure
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env and add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# Run
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000
```

The API will be available at `http://localhost:8000`. Swagger docs at `http://localhost:8000/docs`.

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open `http://localhost:3000`.

### Docker

```bash
# Set your API key
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your-key-here

docker compose up --build
```

## Usage

### Scraping Portfolio Data

The database starts with 20 pre-seeded investors (YC, Sequoia, a16z, etc.). To scrape their portfolios:

1. **Via API:** `POST http://localhost:8000/api/scrape/{investor_id}` to scrape one investor, or `POST http://localhost:8000/api/scrape/all` for all.
2. **Via UI:** Go to an investor's detail page and click "Scrape Now".

### Search & Browse

- **Full-text search** across company names, descriptions, and sectors
- **Filter** by sector, stage, and investor
- **Toggle** between grid and table views
- **Dark/light mode** support

## API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|----------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/companies` | Search/list companies (supports `q`, `sector`, `stage`, `investor`, `sort`, `page`) |
| GET | `/api/companies/{slug}` | Company detail with investors |
| GET | `/api/investors` | List all investors |
| GET | `/api/investors/{id}` | Investor detail with portfolio count |
| GET | `/api/stats` | Dashboard statistics |
| GET | `/api/filters` | Available filter values |
| POST | `/api/scrape/{id}` | Trigger scrape for one investor |
| POST | `/api/scrape/all` | Trigger full scrape cycle |
| GET | `/api/scrape/logs` | Recent scrape logs |

## How It Works

1. **Playwright** loads portfolio pages in a headless browser, handling JS rendering and lazy loading
2. **BeautifulSoup** strips the HTML down to essential content (removes scripts, styles, navigation)
3. **Claude API** extracts structured company data (name, sector, stage, description, etc.) from the cleaned HTML
4. **Normalizer** deduplicates companies using domain matching and fuzzy name matching, then upserts into SQLite
5. **FTS5** provides fast full-text search across the aggregated data

## Cost

Scraping all 20 investors costs roughly $0.50–$2.00 in Claude API usage per cycle. YC's large portfolio may cost more due to HTML size.

content.text:
All phases complete. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Project: `/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/`**

### Backend (Python/FastAPI)
- **`config.py`** — Settings via pydantic-settings, loads from `.env`
- **`database/`** — SQLAlchemy models (4 tables), async SQLite with WAL mode, FTS5 virtual table with sync triggers, 20 pre-seeded investors
- **`scraper/engine.py`** — Playwright-based page fetcher with scroll-to-load, HTML preprocessing with BeautifulSoup, chunking for large pages
- **`scraper/extractor.py`** — Claude API integration that extracts structured company JSON from raw HTML
- **`scraper/normalizer.py`** — Domain-based + fuzzy name deduplication, upsert logic
- **`scraper/scheduler.py`** — Full pipeline orchestration + APScheduler for weekly crons
- **`api/routes.py`** — Full REST API with FTS5 search, filters, pagination, manual scrape triggers
- **`main.py`** — FastAPI app with CORS

### Frontend (Next.js 14)
- **Main page** — Search bar, filter sidebar (sector/stage), grid/table toggle, pagination
- **Company detail** — Full info + "backed by" investor list
- **Investors page** — Grid with type filter (VC/Accelerator)
- **Investor detail** — Portfolio view + "Scrape Now" button
- **Dark/light mode** via next-themes

### Infrastructure
- Docker Compose setup (backend + frontend)
- `.gitignore`, `.env.example`, `README.md`

### To get started:
```bash
cd vc-aggregator/backend
cp .env.example .env  # Add your ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
source venv/bin/activate
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8000

# In another terminal:
cd vc-aggregator/frontend
npm run dev
```

Then trigger scraping via `POST http://localhost:8000/api/scrape/all` or use the UI.

content.text:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a "VC & Accelerator Portfolio Aggregator" full-stack web application. This is a greenfield project in an empty directory at "/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code".

The user has provided an extremely detailed specification. Here's the summary of key decisions already made:

**Tech Stack:**
- Backend: Python + FastAPI
- Scraping: Playwright (JS-rendered) + BeautifulSoup (static)
- LLM extraction: Claude API (Anthropic SDK) with claude-sonnet-4-20250514
- Database: SQLite + SQLAlchemy ORM
- Frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router) + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
- Scheduling: APScheduler

**Project Structure:**
```
vc-aggregator/
├── backend/
│   ├── main.py
│   ├── config.py
│   ├── database/
│   │   ├── models.py
│   │   ├── connection.py
│   │   └── seed_sources.py
│   ├── scraper/
│   │   ├── engine.py
│   │   ├── extractor.py
│   │   ├── normalizer.py
│   │   └── scheduler.py
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── routes.py
│   │   └── schemas.py
│   └── requirements.txt
├── frontend/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── page.tsx
│   │   ├── company/[slug]/page.tsx
│   │   ├── investors/page.tsx
│   │   ├── investors/[id]/page.tsx
│   │   └── layout.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── SearchBar.tsx
│   │   ├── CompanyCard.tsx
│   │   ├── FilterSidebar.tsx
│   │   ├── InvestorGrid.tsx
│   │   └── DataTable.tsx
│   └── package.json
└── README.md
```

**Database:** 4 tables - investors, companies, investments, scrape_logs (full schema provided with all columns)

**Key Innovation:** Claude API extracts structured company data from raw HTML instead of custom parsers per site.

**API Endpoints:** Full REST API with search, filtering, pagination, manual scrape triggers, and scrape logs.

**Frontend:** 4 pages - main search, company detail, investors list, investor detail. Clean/minimal design with dark/light mode.

**Build Order (4 phases):**
1. Data pipeline (DB, seed data, scraper, extractor, normalizer)
2. API layer (FastAPI endpoints, FTS5 search)
3. Frontend (Next.js pages + components)
4. Polish (scheduler, logs viewer, dark mode, Docker)

**Important Details:**
- Rate limiting: 2-3s delays between scrapes, respect robots.txt, random user agents
- Pagination handling: Playwright scroll-to-bottom, discover API endpoints
- Deduplication: website domain as primary key, fuzzy name matching (thefuzz library)
- Error handling: per-investor try/except, log failures
- Seed data: 20 VC/accelerator portfolio URLs provided (YC, Techstars, 500 Global, Sequoia, a16z, etc.)

Please design the implementation plan with:
1. Exact file creation order
2. Key implementation details for each file
3. Dependencies between components
4. Testing strategy at each phase
5. Any potential issues or considerations (e.g., Claude API token limits for large HTML pages, Playwright installation, CORS setup between FastAPI and Next.js)
6. Consider that SQLite FTS5 needs a virtual table setup
7. Consider how to handle very large portfolio pages (YC has thousands of companies)

Focus on practical, buildable steps. The user wants this built, not just planned.

async def fetch_with_httpx(url: str) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using plain HTTP (for static pages)."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with httpx: {url}")

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(
        headers={"User-Agent": random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS)},
        follow_redirects=True,
        timeout=30.0,
    ) as client:
        response = await client.get(url)
        response.raise_for_status()
        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(response.text)} chars from {url}")
        return response.text


def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
    """Strip unnecessary HTML elements to reduce token count before sending to Claude."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, "html.parser")

    # Remove non-content elements
    for tag in soup(
        ["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript",
         "meta", "link", "iframe", "video", "audio", "source", "picture"]
    ):
        tag.decompose()

    # Strip most attributes, keep only structurally useful ones
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        allowed = {}

content.content:

    92→        for attr in ("href", "src", "alt", "class", "data-company", "data-name"):
    93→            val = tag.get(attr)
    94→            if val:
    95→                # Truncate long class lists
    96→                if attr == "class" and isinstance(val, list):
    97→                    val = " ".join(val[:3])
    98→                allowed[attr] = val
    99→        tag.attrs = allowed
   100→
   101→    # Remove empty tags (except br, hr, img)
   102→    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
   103→        if tag.name not in ("br", "hr", "img") and not tag.get_text(strip=True) and not tag.find("img"):
   104→            tag.decompose()
   105→
   106→    cleaned = str(soup)
   107→    logger.info(f"Preprocessed HTML: {len(raw_html)} -> {len(cleaned)} chars")
   108→    return cleaned
   109→
   110→

content.text: The "remove empty tags" step is recursively removing everything — if a parent contains only children that have no text (like divs wrapping links), they all get removed. Let me remove that overly aggressive step:

import asyncio
import logging
import random

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

from config import settings

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def fetch_with_playwright(
    url: str,
    scroll: bool = True,
    max_scrolls: int = 50,
    wait_after_scroll_ms: int = 1500,
) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using headless Chromium, handling JS rendering and lazy loading."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with Playwright: {url}")

    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(
            user_agent=random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS),
            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
        )
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Block heavy resources to speed up loading
        await page.route(
            "**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,svg,woff,woff2,ttf,eot,mp4,webm,webp,ico}",
            lambda route: route.abort(),
        )

        try:
            await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=60000)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Navigation timeout/error for {url}: {e}")
            # Still try to get whatever loaded
            pass

        if scroll:
            prev_height = 0
            for i in range(max_scrolls):
                curr_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight")
                if curr_height == prev_height:
                    break
                await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
                await page.wait_for_timeout(wait_after_scroll_ms)
                prev_height = curr_height
                if i > 0 and i % 10 == 0:
                    logger.info(f"Scrolled {i} times, page height: {curr_height}")

        html = await page.content()
        await browser.close()

        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(html)} chars from {url}")
        return html


async def fetch_with_httpx(url: str) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using plain HTTP (for static pages)."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with httpx: {url}")

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(
        headers={"User-Agent": random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS)},
        follow_redirects=True,
        timeout=30.0,
    ) as client:
        response = await client.get(url)
        response.raise_for_status()
        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(response.text)} chars from {url}")
        return response.text


def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
    """Strip unnecessary HTML elements to reduce token count before sending to Claude."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, "html.parser")

    # Remove non-content elements
    for tag in soup(
        ["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript",
         "meta", "link", "iframe", "video", "audio", "source", "picture"]
    ):
        tag.decompose()

    # Strip most attributes, keep only structurally useful ones
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        allowed = {}
        for attr in ("href", "src", "alt", "class", "data-company", "data-name"):
            val = tag.get(attr)
            if val:
                # Truncate long class lists
                if attr == "class" and isinstance(val, list):
                    val = " ".join(val[:3])
                allowed[attr] = val
        tag.attrs = allowed

    # Remove empty tags (except br, hr, img)
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        if tag.name not in ("br", "hr", "img") and not tag.get_text(strip=True) and not tag.find("img"):
            tag.decompose()

    cleaned = str(soup)
    logger.info(f"Preprocessed HTML: {len(raw_html)} -> {len(cleaned)} chars")
    return cleaned


def chunk_html(html: str, max_chars: int | None = None) -> list[str]:
    """Split HTML into chunks if it exceeds max_chars."""
    if max_chars is None:
        max_chars = settings.MAX_HTML_CHARS

    if len(html) <= max_chars:
        return [html]

    chunks = []
    # Try to split at div boundaries
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.find("body") or soup

    current_chunk = ""
    for child in body.children:
        child_str = str(child)
        if len(current_chunk) + len(child_str) > max_chars and current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk)
            current_chunk = child_str
        else:
            current_chunk += child_str

    if current_chunk:
        chunks.append(current_chunk)

    # If we couldn't split meaningfully, fall back to character splits
    if not chunks:
        for i in range(0, len(html), max_chars):
            chunks.append(html[i : i + max_chars])

    logger.info(f"Split HTML into {len(chunks)} chunks")
    return chunks


async def rate_limit_delay():
    """Random delay between scraping requests."""
    delay = random.uniform(settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN, settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MAX)
    await asyncio.sleep(delay)

content.text: The problem is that the body has one giant div child. The chunking only splits at top-level body children. I need to recursively split large elements and also reduce `MAX_HTML_CHARS` significantly since HTML is ~3x tokens:

input.new_string:
def _collect_leaf_elements(element) -> list[str]:
    """Recursively collect string representations of reasonably-sized elements."""
    from bs4 import NavigableString, Tag

    s = str(element)
    # If this element is small enough, return it as-is
    if len(s) <= 50_000:
        return [s]

    # If it's a Tag with children, recurse into children
    if isinstance(element, Tag):
        parts = []
        for child in element.children:
            parts.extend(_collect_leaf_elements(child))
        return parts

    # NavigableString that's huge — just split by chars
    return [s[i : i + 50_000] for i in range(0, len(s), 50_000)]


def chunk_html(html: str, max_chars: int | None = None) -> list[str]:
    """Split HTML into chunks if it exceeds max_chars."""
    if max_chars is None:
        max_chars = settings.MAX_HTML_CHARS

    if len(html) <= max_chars:
        return [html]

    # Parse and collect leaf-level elements
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.find("body") or soup
    parts = _collect_leaf_elements(body)

    # Merge parts into chunks up to max_chars
    chunks = []
    current_chunk = ""
    for part in parts:
        if len(current_chunk) + len(part) > max_chars and current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk)
            current_chunk = part
        else:
            current_chunk += part

    if current_chunk:
        chunks.append(current_chunk)

    # Filter out tiny chunks (< 100 chars, likely whitespace)
    chunks = [c for c in chunks if len(c.strip()) >= 100]

    if not chunks:
        # Fallback: character-level splits
        for i in range(0, len(html), max_chars):
            chunks.append(html[i : i + max_chars])

    logger.info(f"Split HTML into {len(chunks)} chunks (sizes: {[len(c) for c in chunks]})")
    return chunks

        return [html]

    # Parse and collect leaf-level elements
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.find("body") or soup
    parts = _collect_leaf_elements(body)

    # Merge parts into chunks up to max_chars
    chunks = []
    current_chunk = ""
    for part in parts:
        if len(current_chunk) + len(part) > max_chars and current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk)
            current_chunk = part
        else:
            current_chunk += part

    if current_chunk:
        chunks.append(current_chunk)

    # Filter out tiny chunks (< 100 chars, likely whitespace)
    chunks = [c for c in chunks if len(c.strip()) >= 100]

    if not chunks:
        # Fallback: character-level splits
        for i in range(0, len(html), max_chars):
            chunks.append(html[i : i + max_chars])

    logger.info(f"Split HTML into {len(chunks)} chunks (sizes: {[len(c) for c in chunks]})")
    return chunks

import asyncio
import logging
import random

import httpx
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

from config import settings

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def fetch_with_playwright(
    url: str,
    scroll: bool = True,
    max_scrolls: int = 50,
    wait_after_scroll_ms: int = 1500,
) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using headless Chromium, handling JS rendering and lazy loading."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with Playwright: {url}")

    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        context = await browser.new_context(
            user_agent=random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS),
            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
        )
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Block heavy resources to speed up loading
        await page.route(
            "**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,gif,svg,woff,woff2,ttf,eot,mp4,webm,webp,ico}",
            lambda route: route.abort(),
        )

        try:
            await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=60000)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Navigation timeout/error for {url}: {e}")
            # Still try to get whatever loaded
            pass

        if scroll:
            prev_height = 0
            for i in range(max_scrolls):
                curr_height = await page.evaluate("document.body.scrollHeight")
                if curr_height == prev_height:
                    break
                await page.evaluate("window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight)")
                await page.wait_for_timeout(wait_after_scroll_ms)
                prev_height = curr_height
                if i > 0 and i % 10 == 0:
                    logger.info(f"Scrolled {i} times, page height: {curr_height}")

        html = await page.content()
        await browser.close()

        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(html)} chars from {url}")
        return html


async def fetch_with_httpx(url: str) -> str:
    """Fetch a page using plain HTTP (for static pages)."""
    logger.info(f"Fetching with httpx: {url}")

    async with httpx.AsyncClient(
        headers={"User-Agent": random.choice(settings.USER_AGENTS)},
        follow_redirects=True,
        timeout=30.0,
    ) as client:
        response = await client.get(url)
        response.raise_for_status()
        logger.info(f"Fetched {len(response.text)} chars from {url}")
        return response.text


def preprocess_html(raw_html: str) -> str:
    """Strip unnecessary HTML elements to reduce token count before sending to Claude."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(raw_html, "html.parser")

    # Remove non-content elements
    for tag in soup(
        ["script", "style", "nav", "footer", "header", "svg", "noscript",
         "meta", "link", "iframe", "video", "audio", "source", "picture"]
    ):
        tag.decompose()

    # Strip most attributes, keep only structurally useful ones
    for tag in soup.find_all(True):
        allowed = {}
        for attr in ("href", "src", "alt", "class", "data-company", "data-name"):
            val = tag.get(attr)
            if val:
                # Truncate long class lists
                if attr == "class" and isinstance(val, list):
                    val = " ".join(val[:3])
                allowed[attr] = val
        tag.attrs = allowed

    cleaned = str(soup)
    logger.info(f"Preprocessed HTML: {len(raw_html)} -> {len(cleaned)} chars")
    return cleaned


def chunk_html(html: str, max_chars: int | None = None) -> list[str]:
    """Split HTML into chunks if it exceeds max_chars."""
    if max_chars is None:
        max_chars = settings.MAX_HTML_CHARS

    if len(html) <= max_chars:
        return [html]

    chunks = []
    # Try to split at div boundaries
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    body = soup.find("body") or soup

    current_chunk = ""
    for child in body.children:
        child_str = str(child)
        if len(current_chunk) + len(child_str) > max_chars and current_chunk:
            chunks.append(current_chunk)
            current_chunk = child_str
        else:
            current_chunk += child_str

    if current_chunk:
        chunks.append(current_chunk)

    # If we couldn't split meaningfully, fall back to character splits
    if not chunks:
        for i in range(0, len(html), max_chars):
            chunks.append(html[i : i + max_chars])

    logger.info(f"Split HTML into {len(chunks)} chunks")
    return chunks


async def rate_limit_delay():
    """Random delay between scraping requests."""
    delay = random.uniform(settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MIN, settings.SCRAPE_DELAY_MAX)
    await asyncio.sleep(delay)

+    # NavigableString that's huge — just split by chars

import asyncio
from playwright.async_api import async_playwright

URLS = [
    ('First Round Capital', 'https://firstround.com/companies/'),
    ('Founders Fund', 'https://foundersfund.com/portfolio/'),
    ('Kleiner Perkins', 'https://www.kleinerperkins.com/portfolio'),
    ('Antler', 'https://www.antler.co/portfolio'),
    ('Index Ventures', 'https://www.indexventures.com/companies/'),
]

async def check(name, url):
    async with async_playwright() as p:
        browser = await p.chromium.launch(headless=True)
        page = await browser.new_page()
        try:
            await page.goto(url, wait_until='networkidle', timeout=20000)
            text = await page.inner_text('body')
            lines = [l.strip() for l in text.split('\n') if l.strip()]
            # Count how many lines look like company names (short, capitalized)
            company_lines = [l for l in lines if len(l) < 50 and len(l) > 2]
            print(f'{name}: {len(text)} chars, ~{len(company_lines)} potential companies')
            # Show first few lines
            for l in lines[:10]:
                print(f'  {l[:80]}')
        except Exception as e:
            print(f'{name}: ERROR - {e}')
        finally:
            await browser.close()

async def main():
    for name, url in URLS:
        await check(name, url)
        print()

asyncio.run(main())
" 2>&1

input.command:
cd "/Users/canerden/scheduled tasks for claude code/vc-aggregator/backend" && find . -name "__pycache__" -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null; source venv/bin/activate && python3 -c "
import asyncio
from scraper.engine import fetch_with_playwright, preprocess_html, chunk_html
from scraper.extractor import extract_companies

async def test():
    html = await fetch_with_playwright('https://firstround.com/companies/')
    cleaned = preprocess_html(html)
    chunks = chunk_html(cleaned)
    print(f'First Round: {len(html)} raw -> {len(cleaned)} cleaned -> {len(chunks)} chunks')
    companies = await extract_companies(html, 'First Round Capital')
    print(f'Found {len(companies)} companies')
    for c in companies[:10]:
        print(f'  - {c.get(\"name\")}: {c.get(\"sector\", \"?\")} | {c.get(\"description\", \"\")[:60]}')

asyncio.run(test())
" 2>&1

content.text:
Repo created and pushed: **https://github.com/ycanerden/startup-portfolio-aggregator**

Now for **Railway deployment** — Railway works best with monorepo setups. You'll need two services: one for the backend and one for the frontend.

Here's how to deploy:

1. **Go to https://railway.com** and create a new project
2. **Add the backend service:**
   - Click "New Service" > "GitHub Repo" > select `startup-portfolio-aggregator`
   - Set **Root Directory** to `backend`
   - Add environment variables:
     - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` = your key
     - `DATABASE_URL` = `sqlite+aiosqlite:///./vc_aggregator.db`
     - `ENABLE_SCHEDULER` = `false`
   - Railway will auto-detect the Dockerfile

3. **Add the frontend service:**
   - Click "New Service" > "GitHub Repo" > same repo
   - Set **Root Directory** to `frontend`
   - Add env var: `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` = the backend's Railway URL (e.g., `https://your-backend.up.railway.app`)

file.content:
FROM node:22-alpine AS base

FROM base AS deps
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

FROM base AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM base AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1

RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs
RUN adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs

COPY --from=builder /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static

USER nextjs

EXPOSE 3000
ENV PORT=3000

CMD ["node", "server.js"]

import logging

from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware

from api.routes import router
from config import settings
from database.connection import init_db
from scraper.scheduler import setup_scheduler

    level=logging.INFO,
    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
)

app = FastAPI(
    title="VC Portfolio Aggregator",
    description="Scrapes, normalizes, and aggregates startup portfolio data from VC firms and accelerators",
    version="1.0.0",
)

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=[
        "http://localhost:3000",
        "http://127.0.0.1:3000",
    ],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(router)


@app.on_event("startup")
async def startup():
    await init_db()
    if settings.ENABLE_SCHEDULER:
        setup_scheduler()


@app.get("/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok"}

file.content:
FROM python:3.13-slim

# Install system dependencies for Playwright
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    libnss3 libatk1.0-0 libatk-bridge2.0-0 libcups2 libdrm2 \
    libxkbcommon0 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxrandr2 libgbm1 \
    libpango-1.0-0 libasound2 libxshmfence1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
RUN playwright install chromium

COPY . .

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

    libxkbcommon0 libxcomposite1 libxdamage1 libxrandr2 libgbm1 \
    libpango-1.0-0 libasound2 libxshmfence1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt
RUN playwright install chromium

COPY . .

EXPOSE 8000

CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
